---
title: "fixing data and topic modelling"
author: "Ole Magnus"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Packages

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(stringi)
library(quanteda)
library(topicmodels)
library(stopwords)
library(ggplot2)
```

# LOAD BASE DATASETS

```{r}

# Convert to data.table
mentions  <- as.data.table(lenta_experts_trf_1999_2024_mentions)
articles  <- as.data.table(lenta_news_1999_2024_translated_all)

# Ensure date formats
mentions[, DateParsed := as.Date(DateParsed)]
articles[, date := as.Date(date)]

# Sanity check: ID alignment

all(unique(mentions$row_id) %in% unique(articles$orig_row_id))


```

# a better filter

```{r}

library(data.table)

setDT(mentions)

# Ensure numeric
mentions[, mention_country_confidence := as.numeric(mention_country_confidence)]

# Keep only usable country evidence
country_evidence <- mentions[
  !is.na(expert_id) &
  mention_country != "Unknown" &
  !is.na(mention_country_confidence)
]

# Expert master country = country with highest confidence
# (If ties, pick the one appearing most often among tied max)
expert_master_country <- country_evidence[
  ,
  {
    maxc <- max(mention_country_confidence, na.rm = TRUE)
    top <- .SD[mention_country_confidence == maxc]
    # tie-break: most frequent among the max-confidence mentions
    best_country <- top[, .N, by = mention_country][order(-N)][1, mention_country]
    .(expert_country_master = best_country, expert_country_maxconf = maxc)
  },
  by = expert_id
]

CONF_THRESH <- 0.70

mentions <- merge(
  mentions,
  expert_master_country,
  by = "expert_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)

mentions[, mention_country_confidence := as.numeric(mention_country_confidence)]

mentions[, country_final := fifelse(
  mention_country != "Unknown" & !is.na(mention_country_confidence) & mention_country_confidence >= CONF_THRESH,
  mention_country,
  expert_country_master
)]

# If still NA, set to Unknown for clarity
mentions[is.na(country_final), country_final := "Unknown"]

mentions[, is_foreign_final := country_final != "Russia" & country_final != "Unknown"]

mentions <- mentions[
  person != "Unknown" &                   # still require named person if you want
  country_final != "Unknown"              # but allow imputed countries
]

fwrite(mentions, "mentions_dataset_for_sentiment.csv")
```

# FILTER VALID EXPERT MENTIONS

# DIRECT MERGE VIA ROW_ID (NO STRING MATCHING)

```{r}

final_data <- merge(
  mentions,
  articles[, .(orig_row_id, text_en, title_en, date)],
  by.x = "row_id",
  by.y = "orig_row_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# Remove any unexpected NAs
final_data <- final_data[!is.na(text_en)]
```

# BUILD ARTICLE-LEVEL DATASET

```{r}

article_level <- final_data[
  ,
  .(
    DateParsed = first(DateParsed),
    text_en    = first(text_en),
    title_en   = first(title_en),
    n_mentions = .N,
    mention_sentences = paste(unique(sentence), collapse = " ||| ")
  ),
  by = row_id
]

article_level[, doc_text := paste(title_en, text_en, sep = ". ")]

fwrite(article_level, "article_level_clean.csv")
```

# HARD-NEWS TOPIC MODELLING (TEST 4 CLEAN VERSION)

## Hard-News Filtering

```{r}


article_level[, title_lc := tolower(title_en)]

exclude_pat <- paste0(
  "(",
  paste(c(
    "match","team","league","football","hockey","film","movie","actor",
    "concert","weather","forecast","recipe","diet","fashion","tips"
  ), collapse="|"),
  ")"
)

article_level <- article_level[!stri_detect_regex(title_lc, exclude_pat)]
article_level[, title_lc := NULL]
```

## Tokenization

```{r}

corp <- corpus(article_level$doc_text)

toks <- tokens(
  corp,
  remove_punct = TRUE,
  remove_symbols = TRUE,
  remove_numbers = TRUE
)

toks <- tokens_tolower(toks)

news_stopwords <- c(
  "said","according","earlier","new","one","two",
  "also","can","may","year","years","reported"
)

toks <- tokens_remove(toks, c(stopwords("en"), news_stopwords))

keep_docs <- lengths(toks) > 40
toks <- toks[keep_docs]
article_level <- article_level[keep_docs]
```

## DFM + Trim

```{r}

dfm_all <- dfm_trim(
  dfm_all,
  min_docfreq = 30,
  max_docfreq = 0.6 * ndoc(dfm_all),
  docfreq_type = "count"
)

non_empty <- ntoken(dfm_all) > 0

dfm_all <- dfm_all[non_empty, ]

article_level <- article_level[non_empty]
```

New filtering with better stopwords:

```{r}

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 1) HARD-NEWS FILTER (Title-based exclusion)
# ----------------------------------------------------------

article_level[, title_lc := tolower(title_en)]

exclude_pat <- paste0(
  "(",
  paste(c(
    # sports
    "match","matches","team","club","coach","league","championship","tournament","football","soccer",
    "hockey","nba","nhl","fifa","olympic","olympics","race","grand prix",
    # culture/entertainment
    "film","movie","cinema","actor","actress","director","singer","concert","album","tv","series",
    "show","theater","theatre","book","novel","literature","festival","premiere","museum",
    # weather
    "weather","forecast","temperature","storm","snow","rain","wind","heat","cold",
    # lifestyle / advice
    "recipe","cook","diet","fitness","beauty","fashion","psychologist","relationship",
    "children","parent","pregnan","doctor recommends","tips","how to"
  ), collapse = "|"),
  ")"
)

article_level <- article_level[!stri_detect_regex(title_lc, exclude_pat)]
article_level[, title_lc := NULL]

gc()

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Tokenization
# ----------------------------------------------------------

corp <- corpus(article_level$doc_text)

toks <- tokens(
  corp,
  remove_punct = TRUE,
  remove_symbols = TRUE,
  remove_numbers = TRUE
)

toks <- tokens_tolower(toks)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Stopwords (stronger cleaning)
# ----------------------------------------------------------

news_stopwords <- c(
  "said","according","earlier","new","one","two","first",
  "also","can","may","time","year","years","reported",
  "found","known","made","way","need","even","many","get","help",
  "people","url","now","however","even","still","today"
)

expert_role_stopwords <- c(
  "expert","experts","specialist","specialists","professor","professors",
  "doctor","doctors","scientist","scientists","researcher","researchers",
  "analyst","analysts","economist","economists","lawyer","lawyers",
  "institute","university","academy","center","centre","department",
  "ministry","director","head"
)

reporting_verbs <- c(
  "said","told","explained","noted","added","stated","called",
  "announced","commented","emphasized","pointed","claimed"
)

toks <- tokens_remove(
  toks,
  c(stopwords("en"),
    news_stopwords,
    expert_role_stopwords,
    reporting_verbs)
)

# Remove ultra-short tokens
toks <- tokens_keep(toks, pattern = "^[a-z]{3,}$", valuetype = "regex")

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Remove short documents AFTER cleaning
# ----------------------------------------------------------

keep_docs <- lengths(toks) > 50

toks <- toks[keep_docs]
article_level <- article_level[keep_docs]

gc()

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 5) DFM + Trim
# ----------------------------------------------------------

dfm_all <- dfm(toks)

dfm_all <- dfm_trim(
  dfm_all,
  min_docfreq = 30,
  max_docfreq = 0.6 * ndoc(dfm_all),
  docfreq_type = "count"
)

non_empty <- ntoken(dfm_all) > 0
dfm_all <- dfm_all[non_empty, ]
article_level <- article_level[non_empty]

gc()

```

## LDA

```{r}
dtm <- convert(dfm_all, to = "topicmodels")

K <- 15
set.seed(123)

lda_fit <- LDA(
  dtm,
  k = K,
  method = "Gibbs",
  control = list(iter=1200, burnin=200, thin=100, seed=123)
)

theta <- posterior(lda_fit)$topics
theta_dt <- as.data.table(theta)
setnames(theta_dt, paste0("topic_", 1:K))

hardnews_topics_k15_fast <- cbind(
  article_level[, .(row_id, DateParsed, n_mentions)],
  theta_dt
)
top_terms <- terms(lda_fit, 15)
print(top_terms)

setnames(hardnews_topics_k15_fast,
         old = paste0("topic_", 1:15),
         new = c(
           "macro_economy",
           "media_society",
           "general_news",
           "housing_market",
           "opinion_discourse",
           "covid_health",
           "development_education",
           "energy_sanctions",
           "military_technology",
           "information_services",
           "kremlin_governance",
           "courts_crime",
           "regions_local",
           "ukraine_war",
           "international_geopolitics"
         ))

fwrite(hardnews_topics_k15_fast, "hardnews_topics_k15_fast.csv")
```

# ISSUE-SPACE COORDINATES

```{r}
dt <- copy(hardnews_topics_k15_fast)

dt[, issue_external :=
      military_technology +
      ukraine_war +
      international_geopolitics]

dt[, issue_internal :=
      kremlin_governance +
      courts_crime +
      regions_local]

dt[, issue_x := issue_external - issue_internal]

dt[, issue_informational :=
      information_services +
      media_society +
      development_education +
      covid_health]

dt[, issue_economic :=
      macro_economy +
      housing_market +
      energy_sanctions]

dt[, issue_y := issue_informational - issue_economic]
```

## MENTION-LEVEL ±2 SENTENCE CONTEXT WINDOWS

```{r}
results_list <- vector("list", nrow(article_level))

for (i in seq_len(nrow(article_level))) {
  
  full_text <- article_level$text_en[i]
  mentions  <- article_level$mention_sentences[i]
  orig_row_id <- article_level$orig_row_id[i]
  
  if (is.na(full_text) || is.na(mentions)) next
  
  sents <- unlist(stri_split_boundaries(full_text, type = "sentence"))
  sents <- stri_trim_both(sents)
  sents <- sents[nchar(sents) > 0]
  
  mention_list <- unlist(strsplit(mentions, " \\|\\|\\| "))
  mention_list <- stri_trim_both(mention_list)
  
  mention_rows <- list()
  
  for (m in mention_list) {
    
    idx <- which(sents == m)
    if (length(idx) == 0) next
    
    j <- idx[1]
    
    start <- max(1, j - 2)
    end   <- min(length(sents), j + 2)
    
    context_text <- paste(sents[start:end], collapse=" ")
    
    mention_rows[[length(mention_rows)+1]] <- data.table(
      orig_row_id = orig_row_id,
      mention_sentence = m,
      expert_context_text = context_text
    )
  }
  
  if (length(mention_rows) > 0) {
    results_list[[i]] <- rbindlist(mention_rows)
  }
}

expert_mentions_level <- rbindlist(results_list)

fwrite(expert_mentions_level, "expert_mentions_level_hardnews.csv")
```

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(stringi)

setDT(article_level)

# Safety: ensure the key columns exist
stopifnot(all(c("row_id", "text_en", "mention_sentences") %in% names(article_level)))

results_list <- vector("list", nrow(article_level))

for (i in seq_len(nrow(article_level))) {
  
  row_id   <- article_level$row_id[i]
  full_text <- article_level$text_en[i]
  mentions  <- article_level$mention_sentences[i]
  
  if (is.na(full_text) || is.na(mentions)) next
  if (nchar(full_text) < 50 || nchar(mentions) < 5) next
  
  # Split article into sentences
  sents <- unlist(stri_split_boundaries(full_text, type = "sentence"))
  sents <- stri_trim_both(sents)
  sents <- sents[nchar(sents) > 0]
  if (length(sents) == 0) next
  
  # Split mention sentences
  mention_list <- unlist(strsplit(mentions, " \\|\\|\\| "))
  mention_list <- stri_trim_both(mention_list)
  mention_list <- mention_list[nchar(mention_list) > 0]
  if (length(mention_list) == 0) next
  
  mention_rows <- vector("list", length(mention_list))
  out_k <- 0L
  
  for (m in mention_list) {
    
    # Robust match: find sentence(s) that contain m OR where m contains the sentence
    hits1 <- which(stri_detect_fixed(sents, m))
    hits2 <- which(stri_detect_fixed(m, sents))
    idx <- unique(c(hits1, hits2))
    
    if (length(idx) == 0) next
    
    # If multiple hits, choose the closest-length sentence (best match)
    if (length(idx) > 1) {
      lens <- abs(nchar(sents[idx]) - nchar(m))
      j <- idx[which.min(lens)]
    } else {
      j <- idx[1]
    }
    
    # ±2 sentences
    start <- max(1L, j - 2L)
    end   <- min(length(sents), j + 2L)
    
    context_text <- paste(sents[start:end], collapse = " ")
    
    # Cap (optional; keeps embeddings sane)
    if (nchar(context_text) > 3000) {
      context_text <- substr(context_text, 1, 3000)
    }
    
    out_k <- out_k + 1L
    mention_rows[[out_k]] <- data.table(
      row_id = row_id,                 # <-- KEEP THE ID
      mention_sentence = m,
      expert_context_text = context_text
    )
  }
  
  if (out_k > 0L) {
    results_list[[i]] <- rbindlist(mention_rows[1:out_k])
  }
  
  if (i %% 5000 == 0) cat("Processed:", i, "\n")
}

expert_mentions_level <- rbindlist(results_list, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# Diagnostics
cat("Rows:", nrow(expert_mentions_level), "\n")
cat("Unique row_id:", length(unique(expert_mentions_level$row_id)), "\n")
print(summary(nchar(expert_mentions_level$expert_context_text)))

# Save
fwrite(expert_mentions_level, "expert_mentions_level_hardnews.csv")

getwd(
)
```

# Cluster model

```{r}

clusters <- expert_discourse_clusters

setDT(clusters)

cluster_sizes <- clusters[, .N, by = cluster][order(-N)]

print(cluster_sizes)


set.seed(1)

top_clusters <- cluster_sizes$cluster[cluster_sizes$cluster != -1][1:15]

for (c in top_clusters) {

  cat("\n===========================\n")
  cat("CLUSTER", c, "\n")
  cat("===========================\n")

  sample_rows <- clusters[clusters$cluster == c, ]

  sample_rows <- sample_rows[sample(nrow(sample_rows), min(10, nrow(sample_rows))), ]

  for (s in sample_rows$expert_context_text) {
    cat("-", substr(s, 1, 300), "\n\n")
  }
}

mentions_model <- mentions_full[cluster != -1]

mentions_model[, archetype := NA_character_]


```

# Merging cluster, topic and mention

```{r}

library(data.table)

setDT(expert_discourse_clusters)
setDT(mentions_clean)
setDT(hardnews_topics_k15_fast)

mentions_full <- merge(
  expert_discourse_clusters,
  mentions_clean[
    ,
    .(
      row_id,
      sentence,
      expert_id,
      person,
      organization,
      role,
      role_class,
      country_final,
      expert_country_master,
      is_foreign_final,
      is_government_official,
      is_journalist,
      org_type,
      DateParsed
    )
  ],
  by.x = c("row_id", "mention_sentence"),
  by.y = c("row_id", "sentence"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

mentions_full <- merge(
  mentions_full,
  hardnews_topics_k15_fast,
  by = "row_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)

mentions_model <- mentions_full[cluster != -1]

mentions_model[, archetype := NA_character_]


mentions_model[cluster %in% c(7),
               archetype := "scientific_authority"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(37),
               archetype := "military_analysis"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(44),
               archetype := "security_threat"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(22),
               archetype := "energy_geopolitics"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(46),
               archetype := "financial_markets"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(24,19),
               archetype := "domestic_economy"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(41,38),
               archetype := "legal_governance"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(52),
               archetype := "cyber_security"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(9),
               archetype := "health_advice"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(42),
               archetype := "political_legitimation"]

mentions_model[cluster %in% c(1,5,33,35),
               archetype := "general_expert_commentary"]

mentions_model[is.na(archetype), archetype := "other_discourse"]

table(mentions_model$archetype, useNA = "ifany")

nrow(mentions_full)

table(is.na(mentions_full$cluster))

table(is.na(mentions_full$person))

table(is.na(mentions_full$macro_economy))

mentions_full[, year := year(DateParsed.x)]

table(mentions_full$cluster, mentions_full$is_foreign_final)

prop.table(table(mentions_model$archetype, mentions_model$is_foreign_final), 1)


glm(
  is_foreign_final ~ archetype,
  family = binomial,
  data = mentions_model
)
glm(
  is_foreign_final ~ archetype +
  military_technology +
  international_geopolitics +
  energy_sanctions +
  macro_economy + 
  kremlin_governance,
  family = binomial,
  data = mentions_model
)

library(ggplot2)

arch <- mentions_model[
  ,
  .(foreign_share = mean(is_foreign_final)),
  by = archetype
]

ggplot(arch, aes(x = reorder(archetype, foreign_share),
                 y = foreign_share)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = "Expert discourse archetype",
    y = "Share foreign experts"
  )


arch <- mentions_model[
  ,
  .(foreign_share = mean(is_foreign_final)),
  by = archetype
]

ggplot(arch,
       aes(x = reorder(archetype, foreign_share),
           y = foreign_share)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

```

# Mapping issue space + cluster

```{r}

dt <- copy(mentions_model)

dt[, issue_security :=
      military_technology + international_geopolitics]

dt[, issue_economy :=
      macro_economy + energy_sanctions]

dt[, issue_governance :=
      kremlin_governance]

dt[, issue_information :=
      information_services]

dt[, issue_x := issue_security - issue_governance]
dt[, issue_y := issue_information - issue_economy]

arch_space <- dt[
  ,
  .(
    issue_x = mean(issue_x, na.rm = TRUE),
    issue_y = mean(issue_y, na.rm = TRUE),
    foreign_share = mean(is_foreign_final),
    n = .N
  ),
  by = archetype
]

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(arch_space,
       aes(x = issue_x,
           y = issue_y,
           label = archetype,
           size = n,
           color = foreign_share)) +

  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +

  geom_text(nudge_y = 0.02) +

  scale_color_gradient(low = "steelblue",
                       high = "red") +

  labs(
    x = "Security  ←→  Governance",
    y = "Information  ←→  Economy",
    color = "Foreign expert share",
    size = "Mentions"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()

```

## Temporal shifts in expert discourse.

```{r}

arch_time <- mentions_model[
  ,
  .N,
  by = .(year, archetype)
]

arch_time[, share := N / sum(N), by = year]

ggplot(arch_time,
       aes(x = year,
           y = share,
           color = archetype)) +

  geom_line(size = 1) +

  theme_minimal() +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Share of expert discourse",
    color = "Archetype"
  )

foreign_time <- mentions_model[
  ,
  .(foreign_share = mean(is_foreign_final)),
  by = year
]

ggplot(foreign_time,
       aes(x = year,
           y = foreign_share)) +

  geom_line(size = 1.2,
            color = "darkred") +

  theme_minimal() +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Foreign expert share"
  )

arch_foreign_time <- mentions_model[
  ,
  .(foreign_share = mean(is_foreign_final)),
  by = .(year, archetype)
]

ggplot(arch_foreign_time,
       aes(x = year,
           y = foreign_share,
           color = archetype)) +

  geom_line() +

  theme_minimal() +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Foreign expert share"
  )
```

# Merging for full dataset

```{r}

sentiment <- mentions_directed_sentiment_full

topics <- as.data.table(hardnews_topics_k15_fast)

master_dataset <- merge(
  sentiment,
  topics,
  by = "row_id",
  all.x = TRUE
)

sum(is.na(master_dataset$ukraine_war))

topic_cols <- c(
"courts_crime",
"covid_health",
"development_education",
"energy_sanctions",
"general_news",
"housing_market",
"information_services",
"international_geopolitics",
"kremlin_governance",
"macro_economy",
"media_society",
"military_technology",
"opinion_discourse",
"regions_local",
"ukraine_war"
)

master_dataset[topic_cols] <- lapply(master_dataset[topic_cols], function(x) {
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  x
})

master_dataset$dominant_topic <- topic_cols[
  max.col(master_dataset[topic_cols], ties.method = "first")
]

table(master_dataset$dominant_topic)

sum(rowSums(master_dataset[topic_cols]) == 0)

master_dataset$dominant_topic[rowSums(master_dataset[topic_cols]) == 0] <- "no_topic"

master_dataset$topic_group <- "other"

master_dataset$topic_group[
master_dataset$dominant_topic %in% c(
"ukraine_war",
"international_geopolitics",
"military_technology",
"energy_sanctions"
)] <- "geopolitics"

master_dataset$topic_group[
master_dataset$dominant_topic %in% c(
"macro_economy",
"housing_market"
)] <- "economy"

master_dataset$topic_group[
master_dataset$dominant_topic %in% c(
"kremlin_governance",
"opinion_discourse",
"media_society"
)] <- "politics"

master_dataset$topic_group[
master_dataset$dominant_topic == "courts_crime"
] <- "crime"


table(master_dataset$topic_group)


master_dataset$anti_west_vs_russia <-
master_dataset$any_anti_west - master_dataset$any_anti_russia

aggregate(anti_west_vs_russia ~ topic_group,
data = master_dataset,
mean)

write.csv(
  master_dataset,
  "C:/Users/olemga/OneDrive - PRIO/Documents/MA Thesis/Methods/Data/expert_topic_sentiment_master.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

master_dataset$year <- as.integer(format(as.Date(master_dataset$DateParsed.x), "%Y"))

model1 <- lm(
anti_west_vs_russia ~
is_foreign_final +
topic_group +
year,
data = master_dataset
)

summary(model1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN AND STANDARDIZE MASTER DATASET
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Standardize variable names
names(master_dataset) <- tolower(names(master_dataset))

# 2. Create clean date variable and remove merge duplicates
master_dataset$date <- as.Date(master_dataset$dateparsed.x)

master_dataset$dateparsed.x <- NULL
master_dataset$dateparsed.y <- NULL

# 3. Create year variable
master_dataset$year <- as.integer(format(master_dataset$date, "%Y"))

# 4. Rename key variables for clarity
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "sentence"] <- "mention_sentence"
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "country_final"] <- "expert_country"
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "is_foreign_final"] <- "foreign_expert"

# 5. Topic columns
topic_cols <- c(
"courts_crime",
"covid_health",
"development_education",
"energy_sanctions",
"general_news",
"housing_market",
"information_services",
"international_geopolitics",
"kremlin_governance",
"macro_economy",
"media_society",
"military_technology",
"opinion_discourse",
"regions_local",
"ukraine_war"
)

# 6. Replace missing topic weights with 0
master_dataset[topic_cols] <- lapply(master_dataset[topic_cols], function(x) {
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  x
})

# 7. Create dominant topic
master_dataset$dominant_topic <- topic_cols[
  max.col(master_dataset[topic_cols], ties.method = "first")
]

# 8. Label rows without topic modelling
master_dataset$dominant_topic[rowSums(master_dataset[topic_cols]) == 0] <- "no_topic"

# 9. Rename key sentiment variables
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "any_anti_west"] <- "anti_west"
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "any_anti_russia"] <- "anti_russia"
names(master_dataset)[names(master_dataset) == "any_anti_ukraine"] <- "anti_ukraine"

# 10. Recreate asymmetry index
master_dataset$anti_west_vs_russia <- master_dataset$anti_west - master_dataset$anti_russia

# 11. Convert important variables to proper types
master_dataset$foreign_expert <- as.integer(master_dataset$foreign_expert)
master_dataset$topic_group <- factor(master_dataset$topic_group)
master_dataset$dominant_topic <- factor(master_dataset$dominant_topic)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# DATASET IS NOW CLEAN
# ------------------------------------------------------------

```

# Analyses:

share of domestic vs. foreign

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

master_dataset %>%
  count(foreign_expert) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(foreign_expert, labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")), y = share)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    title = "Foreign and domestic experts in the corpus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = year, y = share)) +

  # Light raw line (de-emphasized)
  geom_line(color = "#2b6cb0", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.4) +

  # Strong smoothed trend
  geom_smooth(method = "loess",
              se = FALSE,
              color = "black",
              size = 1.2) +

  # Reference line at 50%
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5,
             linetype = "dotted",
             color = "grey40") +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     limits = c(0.3, 0.7)) +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    title = "Share of foreign experts over time",
    subtitle = "Foreign experts consistently account for roughly half of expert mentions"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

Counts over time

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)

# Prepare data
plot_counts <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month, foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(type = ifelse(foreign_expert == 1, "Foreign", "Domestic"))

# Get dynamic y-axis max for annotations
y_max <- max(plot_counts$n, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot

ggplot(plot_counts, aes(x = month, y = n, color = type)) +

  # Raw monthly lines (light)
  geom_line(size = 0.35, alpha = 0.35) +

  # Smoothed trend
  geom_smooth(
    se = FALSE,
    size = 1.2,
    span = 0.05
  ) +

  # Vertical lines for key events
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2014-01-01"),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2020-03-01"),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  # Labels for events
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = as.Date("2014-01-01"),
    y = y_max * 0.99,
    label = "Editorial change",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = as.Date("2020-03-01"),
    y = y_max * 0.99,
    label = "COVID-19",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
    y = y_max * 0.99,
    label = "Ukraine invasion",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 4,
    color = "grey30"
  ) +

  # Colors
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Domestic" = "#b04a4a",
      "Foreign"  = "#2b6cb0"
    )
  ) +

  # Y-axis with extra space at top
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))
  ) +

  # Labels
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of expert mentions",
    color = NULL,
    title = "Number of foreign and domestic expert mentions over time",
    subtitle = "Monthly counts with smoothed trends"
  ) +

  # Theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

Sentiment by target actor

```{r}
master_dataset %>%
  summarise(
    anti_russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "target", values_to = "rate") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = target, y = rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    title = "Negative sentiment by target actor"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



```

Foreign vs domestic experts by target sentiment

```{r}
master_dataset %>%
  group_by(foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(anti_russia, anti_west, anti_ukraine),
    names_to = "target",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = target, y = rate, fill = factor(foreign_expert, labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")))) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Negative sentiment by expert nationality"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

plot_sentiment <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    Russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    Ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    West = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Russia, Ukraine, West),
    names_to = "Target",
    values_to = "Rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")),
    Target = factor(Target,
                    levels = c("Russia", "Ukraine", "West"))
  )

ggplot(plot_sentiment,
       aes(x = Target, y = Rate, fill = Expert)) +

  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Domestic" = "#b04a4a",
      "Foreign"  = "#2b6cb0"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of mentions with negative sentiment",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Negative sentiment by expert nationality",
    subtitle = "Foreign experts are more likely to express negative sentiment toward Western actors"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

Negative sentiment by topic group

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(topic_group) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(anti_russia, anti_west, anti_ukraine),
    names_to = "target",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = topic_group, y = rate, fill = target)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    fill = "Target",
    title = "Negative sentiment across issue areas"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

Negative sentiment by topic group

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(topic_group) %>%
  summarise(mean_asymmetry = mean(anti_west_vs_russia, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(topic_group, mean_asymmetry), y = mean_asymmetry)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Anti-West minus anti-Russia",
    title = "Narrative asymmetry by topic group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Foreign expert use over time

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(foreign_share = mean(foreign_expert, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = foreign_share)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share foreign experts",
    title = "Foreign expert use over time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Anti-West framing over time

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_west = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(anti_west, anti_russia),
    names_to = "target",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = rate, color = target)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    color = NULL,
    title = "Negative sentiment toward West and Russia over time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Alluvial plot

```{r}

library(dplyr)

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(sentiment_category = case_when(
    anti_west == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
    anti_russia == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
    anti_ukraine == 1 ~ "Anti-Ukraine",
    TRUE ~ "Neutral/Other"
  ))

library(ggalluvial)
library(ggplot2)

alluvial_data <- master_dataset %>%
  count(topic_group, foreign_expert, sentiment_category)

ggplot(
  alluvial_data,
  aes(
    axis1 = topic_group,
    axis2 = factor(foreign_expert, labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")),
    axis3 = sentiment_category,
    y = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(aes(fill = sentiment_category), width = 1/12) +
  geom_stratum(width = 1/12, fill = "grey85", color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(stat = "stratum", aes(label = after_stat(stratum))) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"), expand = c(.05, .05)) +
  labs(
    y = "Number of mentions",
    title = "Topic, expert nationality, and directed sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



alluvial_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(sentiment_category != "Neutral/Other") %>%
  count(topic_group, foreign_expert, sentiment_category)



ggplot(
  alluvial_data,
  aes(
    axis1 = topic_group,
    axis2 = factor(foreign_expert, labels = c("Domestic","Foreign")),
    axis3 = sentiment_category,
    y = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(aes(fill = sentiment_category), width = 1/12) +
  geom_stratum(width = 1/12, fill = "grey85", color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(stat = "stratum", aes(label = after_stat(stratum))) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic","Expert origin","Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.05,.05)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Number of mentions",
    title = "Directed sentiment in expert commentary"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


```

Issue space coordinates

```{r}
issue_space <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic) %>%
  summarise(
    foreign_share = mean(foreign_expert),
    crime = mean(courts_crime),
    economy = mean(macro_economy + housing_market),
    geopolitics = mean(ukraine_war + international_geopolitics + military_technology + energy_sanctions),
    politics = mean(kremlin_governance + opinion_discourse + media_society)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    x_coord = economy - crime,
    y_coord = geopolitics - politics
  )

issue_space <- issue_space %>%
  filter(dominant_topic != "other")

library(ggrepel)

ggplot(issue_space,
       aes(x = x_coord,
           y = y_coord,
           label = dominant_topic,
           color = foreign_share)) +

  geom_point(size = 5) +
  geom_text_repel() +

  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "steelblue",
    high = "darkred",
    name = "Share foreign experts"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Crime  ←→  Economy",
    y = "Domestic politics  ←→  Geopolitics",
    title = "Issue-space map of expert discourse"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

issue_space <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(

    # X dimension: crime vs economy
    x_coord = (macro_economy + housing_market) - courts_crime,

    # Y dimension: geopolitics vs domestic politics
    y_coord = (ukraine_war +
               international_geopolitics +
               military_technology +
               energy_sanctions) -
              (kremlin_governance +
               opinion_discourse +
               media_society)

  )

library(ggplot2)

plot_data <- issue_space

ggplot(plot_data,
       aes(x = x_coord,
           y = y_coord,
           color = factor(foreign_expert))) +

  geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.5) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("steelblue", "darkred"),
    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"),
    name = "Expert origin"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Crime  ←→  Economy",
    y = "Domestic politics  ←→  Geopolitics",
    title = "Issue-space map of expert discourse"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()

ggplot(plot_data,
       aes(x = x_coord,
           y = y_coord,
           color = factor(foreign_expert))) +

  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 1) +

  geom_density_2d() +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("steelblue", "darkred"),
    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"),
    name = "Expert origin"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Crime  ←→  Economy",
    y = "Domestic politics  ←→  Geopolitics",
    title = "Issue-space distribution of foreign and domestic experts"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()
```

Map

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(countrycode)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

country_counts <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(!is.na(expert_country_master), expert_country_master != "Unknown") %>%
  count(expert_country_master) %>%
  mutate(
    iso_a3 = countrycode(expert_country_master,
                         origin = "country.name",
                         destination = "iso3c"),
    log_n = log10(n + 1)
  )

world <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "medium",
                                     returnclass = "sf")

map_data <- world %>%
  left_join(country_counts, by = c("iso_a3" = "iso_a3"))

ggplot(map_data) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = log_n),
          color = "white",
          size = 0.1) +

  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#f7fbff",
    high = "#08306b",
    na.value = "grey95",
    breaks = log10(c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000)),
    labels = comma(c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000)),
    name = "Expert mentions"
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Geographic distribution of experts cited in Russian media",
    subtitle = "Counts of expert mentions by country (log-scaled color)"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```

Top foreign expert countries

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    foreign_expert == 1,
    !is.na(expert_country),
    expert_country != "Unknown"
  ) %>%
  count(expert_country, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(expert_country, n), y = n)) +

  geom_col(fill = "#2b6cb0") +   # same tone as map (blue)

  coord_flip() +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +

  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of mentions",
    title = "Most frequently cited foreign expert countries",
    subtitle = "Top 15 countries by number of expert mentions"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),   # cleaner
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  count(topic_group, foreign_expert) %>%
  group_by(foreign_expert) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = topic_group, y = share, fill = factor(foreign_expert, labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")))) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of expert mentions",
    fill = NULL,
    title = "Issue profile of foreign and domestic experts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

heatmap_data <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(topic_group) %>%
  summarise(
    Russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    West = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    Ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(Russia, West, Ukraine), names_to = "target", values_to = "rate")

ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = target, y = topic_group, fill = rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Negative rate",
    title = "Heatmap of negative sentiment by topic and target"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


heatmap_data_dom <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(master_dataset$dominant_topic) %>%
  summarise(
    Russia = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    West = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    Ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(Russia, West, Ukraine), names_to = "target", values_to = "rate")

ggplot(heatmap_data_dom, aes(x = target, y = heatmap_data_dom$`master_dataset$dominant_topic`, fill = rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Negative rate",
    title = "Heatmap of negative sentiment by topic and target"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

heatmap_data <- master_dataset %>%
  count(topic_group, foreign_expert) %>%
  group_by(topic_group) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n))

ggplot(heatmap_data,
       aes(x = topic_group,
           y = factor(foreign_expert,
           labels = c("Domestic","Foreign")),
           fill = share)) +

  geom_tile() +

  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::percent) +

  labs(
    x = "Topic",
    y = "Expert origin",
    fill = "Share",
    title = "Foreign and domestic experts across issue areas"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()
```

# Topic modelling for Tore

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(scales)

topic_cols <- c(
"courts_crime",
"covid_health",
"development_education",
"energy_sanctions",
"general_news",
"housing_market",
"information_services",
"international_geopolitics",
"kremlin_governance",
"macro_economy",
"media_society",
"military_technology",
"opinion_discourse",
"regions_local",
"ukraine_war"
)

topic_distribution <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(topic_cols), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = "topic",
    values_to = "share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic = str_replace_all(topic, "_", " "),
    topic = str_to_title(topic),
    percent = share * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent))

ggplot(topic_distribution,
       aes(x = reorder(topic, percent),
           y = percent)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4C78A8", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percent)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .08)),
    labels = label_percent(scale = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average topic share (%)",
    title = "Topic prevalence in the news corpus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11)
  )

```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(scales)

topic_cols <- c(
"courts_crime",
"covid_health",
"development_education",
"energy_sanctions",
"general_news",
"housing_market",
"information_services",
"international_geopolitics",
"kremlin_governance",
"macro_economy",
"media_society",
"military_technology",
"opinion_discourse",
"regions_local",
"ukraine_war"
)

topic_distribution <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(topic_cols), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = "topic",
    values_to = "share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    
    topic = str_replace_all(topic, "_", " "),
    topic = str_to_title(topic),
    
    percent = share * 100,
    
    group = case_when(
      topic %in% c("Macro Economy","Housing Market","Energy Sanctions") ~ "Economic policy",
      
      topic %in% c("Kremlin Governance","Courts Crime","Regions Local") ~ "Domestic politics",
      
      topic %in% c("Ukraine War","Military Technology","International Geopolitics") ~ "International politics",
      
      topic %in% c("Covid Health","Media Society","Opinion Discourse",
                   "General News","Development Education","Information Services") ~ "Society and information"
    )
    
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(percent))

ggplot(topic_distribution,
       aes(x = reorder(topic, percent),
           y = percent,
           fill = group)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  
  coord_flip() +
  
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percent)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Economic policy" = "#4C78A8",
    "Domestic politics" = "#F58518",
    "International politics" = "#54A24B",
    "Society and information" = "#B279A2"
  )) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .08)),
    labels = label_percent(scale = 1)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average topic share (%)",
    fill = "Topic category",
    title = "Topic prevalence in the Lenta.ru news corpus"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}

topic_map <- data.frame(
topic = topic_cols,
group = c(
"crime",
"other",
"economy",
"geopolitics",
"other",
"economy",
"other",
"geopolitics",
"politics",
"economy",
"politics",
"geopolitics",
"politics",
"other",
"geopolitics"
))

topic_group_dist <- topic_distribution %>%
  left_join(topic_map)

ggplot(topic_group_dist,
       aes(x=group,
           y=share,
           fill=topic)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    x="Topic group",
    y="Average topic weight",
    title="Aggregation of topics into issue groups"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

topic_time <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(topic_cols), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year,
               names_to="topic",
               values_to="share")

ggplot(topic_time,
       aes(x=year,
           y=share,
           color=topic)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Average topic weight",
    title="Topic prevalence over time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

library(treemapify)

topic_sizes <- topic_distribution %>%
  left_join(topic_map)

ggplot(topic_sizes,
       aes(area=share,
           fill=group,
           label=topic)) +
  geom_treemap() +
  geom_treemap_text(
    colour="white",
    place="centre",
    grow=TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title="Topic composition of the corpus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(topic_sizes,
       aes(area=share,
           fill=group,
           label=paste0(topic,"\n",round(share*100,1),"%"))) +

  geom_treemap(color="white") +
  geom_treemap_text(
    colour="white",
    place="centre",
    reflow=TRUE
  ) +

  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Set2") +

  labs(
    title="Topic composition of the corpus",
    fill="Topic group"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  count(dominant_topic) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=reorder(dominant_topic,n), y=n)) +
  geom_col(fill="darkred") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Expert mentions",
    title="Distribution of expert mentions across topics"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Directed sentiment for Tore:

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

sentiment_dist <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(
    anti_russia = sum(anti_russia),
    anti_west = sum(anti_west),
    anti_ukraine = sum(anti_ukraine)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to="target",
    values_to="count"
  )

ggplot(sentiment_dist,
       aes(x=target, y=count)) +
  geom_col(fill="steelblue") +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Number of mentions",
    title="Directed negative sentiment by target actor"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}

sentiment_compare <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(
    Russia = mean(anti_russia),
    West = mean(anti_west),
    Ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to="target",
    values_to="rate"
  )

ggplot(sentiment_compare,
       aes(x=target, y=rate)) +
  geom_col(fill="darkred") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Share of mentions",
    title="Directed sentiment assigns negativity to specific actors"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

sentiment_categories <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(sentiment_category = case_when(
    anti_west == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
    anti_russia == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
    anti_ukraine == 1 ~ "Anti-Ukraine",
    TRUE ~ "Neutral"
  )) %>%
  count(sentiment_category)

ggplot(sentiment_categories,
       aes(x=reorder(sentiment_category, n), y=n)) +
  geom_col(fill="grey50") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Number of sentences",
    title="Distribution of directed sentiment categories"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

targets <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(
    Russia=sum(any_negative_russia),
    West=sum(any_negative_west),
    Ukraine=sum(any_negative_ukraine)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to="target",
    values_to="mentions"
  )

ggplot(targets,
       aes(x=target, y=mentions)) +
  geom_col(fill="firebrick") +
  labs(
    x=NULL,
    y="Mentions",
    title="Targets detected in directed sentiment analysis"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
master_dataset %>%
  filter(anti_west == 1) %>%
  select(mention_sentence) %>%
  slice_sample(n=5)
```

```{r}

library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph pipeline {

graph [layout = dot]

node [shape = box]

A [label='News Articles']
B [label='Topic Modelling']
C [label='Expert Extraction']
D [label='Directed Sentiment']
E [label='Final Dataset']

A -> B -> C -> D -> E

}
")

```

```{r}

library(broom)
library(ggplot2)

model <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

coef_data <- broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)

coef_data <- coef_data %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

ggplot(coef_data,
       aes(x = estimate,
           y = reorder(term, estimate))) +

  geom_point(size = 3) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low,
        xmax = conf.high),
    height = .2
  ) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype = "dashed") +

  labs(
    x = "Coefficient estimate",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Factors associated with anti-West sentiment"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()


```

```{r}

library(ggeffects)

pred <- ggpredict(model, terms = "foreign_expert")

plot(pred) +
  labs(
    x = "Foreign expert",
    y = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment",
    title = "Effect of foreign experts on anti-West sentiment"
  )

pred_topic <- ggpredict(model,
                        terms = c("topic_group","foreign_expert"))

plot(pred_topic) +
  labs(
    x = "Topic group",
    y = "Predicted probability",
    title = "Anti-West sentiment across topics and expert origin"
  )

pred_topic <- ggpredict(model,
                        terms = c("topic_group","foreign_expert"))

plot(pred_topic) +
  labs(
    x = "Topic group",
    y = "Predicted probability",
    title = "Anti-West sentiment across topics and expert origin"
  )
```

```{r}

model1 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

library(ggeffects)

plot(ggpredict(model1, terms = "foreign_expert")) +
  labs(
    x = "Foreign expert",
    y = "Predicted probability",
    title = "Foreign experts and anti-West sentiment"
  )

```

```{r}

model2 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

library(broom)

coef2 <- tidy(model2, conf.int = TRUE)

ggplot(coef2 %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)"),
       aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height=.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype="dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x="Coefficient",
    y=NULL,
    title="Topic effects on anti-West sentiment"
  )

```

```{r}

model3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

plot(ggpredict(model3, terms = c("topic_group","foreign_expert"))) +
  labs(
    x="Topic group",
    y="Predicted probability",
    title="Foreign experts across issue areas"
  )
```

```{r}

model4 <- lm(
  anti_west_vs_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data = master_dataset
)

coef4 <- tidy(model4, conf.int = TRUE)

ggplot(coef4 %>% filter(term!="(Intercept)"),
       aes(x=estimate, y=reorder(term,estimate))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin=conf.low, xmax=conf.high), height=.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title="Drivers of narrative asymmetry"
  )
```

```{r}

model5 <- glm(
  anti_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

model5
```

```{r}

model6 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)


plot(ggpredict(model6, terms="year")) +
  labs(
    y="Predicted probability",
    title="Anti-West sentiment over time"
  )
```

```{r}

model_topic <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + dominant_topic + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

library(broom)
library(dplyr)

coef_topic <- tidy(model_topic, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")


coef_topic <- coef_topic %>%
  mutate(
    term = gsub("dominant_topic", "", term),
    term = gsub("_", " ", term)
  )

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(coef_topic,
       aes(x = estimate,
           y = reorder(term, estimate))) +

  geom_point(size = 3) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low,
        xmax = conf.high),
    height = .2
  ) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40") +

  labs(
    x = "Coefficient estimate",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Anti-West sentiment across dominant topics"
  ) +

  theme_minimal()


coef_topic <- coef_topic %>%
  filter(grepl("topic", term))

library(ggeffects)

pred_topic <- ggpredict(
  model_topic,
  terms = "dominant_topic"
)

plot(pred_topic) +
  labs(
    x = "Dominant topic",
    y = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment",
    title = "Anti-West sentiment by topic"
  )
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

target_data <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    target = case_when(
      any_positive_russia == 1 | any_negative_russia == 1 | any_neutral_russia == 1 ~ "Russia",
      any_positive_west == 1 | any_negative_west == 1 | any_neutral_west == 1 ~ "West",
      any_positive_ukraine == 1 | any_negative_ukraine == 1 | any_neutral_ukraine == 1 ~ "Ukraine",
      TRUE ~ "Other actors / non-target"
    )
  )

counts <- target_data %>%
  count(target)

detected <- counts %>%
  filter(target != "Other actors / non-target") %>%
  mutate(group = "Detected targets")

other <- counts %>%
  filter(target == "Other actors / non-target") %>%
  mutate(group = "Other actors / non-target")

plot_data <- bind_rows(detected, other)

ggplot(plot_data,
       aes(x = group,
           y = n,
           fill = target)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  
 scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Russia" = "#4E79A7",
  "West" = "#76B7B2",
  "Ukraine" = "#F28E2B",
  "Other actors / non-target" = "#D3D3D3"
)) +
  
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of expert mention sentences",
    fill = "Target actor",
    title = "Detection of geopolitical targets in expert commentary"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

sentiment_data <- master_dataset %>%
  summarise(
    Russia_Positive = sum(any_positive_russia),
    Russia_Neutral  = sum(any_neutral_russia),
    Russia_Negative = sum(any_negative_russia),
    
    West_Positive   = sum(any_positive_west),
    West_Neutral    = sum(any_neutral_west),
    West_Negative   = sum(any_negative_west),
    
    Ukraine_Positive = sum(any_positive_ukraine),
    Ukraine_Neutral  = sum(any_neutral_ukraine),
    Ukraine_Negative = sum(any_negative_ukraine)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
  separate(name, into = c("target","sentiment"), sep = "_")

ggplot(sentiment_data,
       aes(x = target,
           y = value,
           fill = sentiment)) +
  
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
           width = 0.6) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Positive" = "#4C78A8",
    "Neutral"  = "#D9D9D9",
    "Negative" = "#7F7F7F"
  )) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Target actor",
    y = "Number of expert mention sentences",
    fill = "Sentiment",
    title = "Sentiment classification across detected geopolitical targets"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

# Plots for writing:

## H1: Foreign expert + topic

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(scales)

# Create clean topic names (without modifying original variable)
master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = recode(dominant_topic,
      "ukraine_war" = "Ukraine war",
      "international_geopolitics" = "International geopolitics",
      "media_society" = "Media and society",
      "no_topic" = "Other / no clear topic",
      "military_technology" = "Military technology",
      "general_news" = "General news reporting",
      "energy_sanctions" = "Energy and sanctions",
      "opinion_discourse" = "Opinion and commentary",
      "macro_economy" = "Macroeconomy",
      "courts_crime" = "Courts and crime",
      "kremlin_governance" = "Kremlin governance",
      "covid_health" = "COVID-19 and health",
      "information_services" = "Information services",
      "housing_market" = "Housing market",
      "regions_local" = "Regional and local issues",
      "development_education" = "Development and education"
    )
  )
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(scales)

# Ensure dominant_topic is a factor and set reference category
master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean)

# OPTIONAL: change reference to a meaningful baseline if you have one
# Example:
# master_dataset$dominant_topic <- relevel(master_dataset$dominant_topic, ref = "Domestic politics")

# Run model
model_h1 <- glm(
  foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# Get predicted probabilities
pred_topic <- ggpredict(model_h1, terms = "dominant_topic_clean")

pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_topic)

# Plot
ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = reorder(x, predicted), y = predicted)) +

  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2b6cb0") +

  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width = 0.2
  ) +

  coord_flip() +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +

  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted probability of foreign expert",
    title = "Foreign expert presence across topics"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic) %>%
  summarise(
    share_foreign = mean(foreign_expert)
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(dominant_topic, share_foreign),
             y = share_foreign)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2b6cb0") +
  coord_flip()
```

## H2: Foreign expert + sentiment

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(scales)

# Run main model
model_sentiment <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + dominant_topic_clean + year,
  data = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# Get predicted probabilities for foreign vs domestic
pred_expert <- ggpredict(model_sentiment, terms = "foreign_expert")

pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_expert)

# Clean labels
pred_df <- pred_df %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(x,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic expert", "Foreign expert"))
  )

# Plot
ggplot(pred_df,
       aes(x = Expert, y = predicted, color = Expert)) +

  geom_point(size = 3) +

  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width = 0.15
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Domestic expert" = "#b04a4a",
      "Foreign expert"  = "#2b6cb0"
    )
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted probability of negative sentiment toward the West",
    title = "Effect of foreign experts on anti-West sentiment"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

# Models
model_west   <- glm(anti_west   ~ foreign_expert + year, data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_russia <- glm(anti_russia ~ foreign_expert + year, data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_ukraine <- glm(anti_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + year, data = master_dataset, family = binomial)

# Extract and prepare coefficients
coef_df <- bind_rows(
  tidy(model_russia)  %>% mutate(Target = "Russia"),
  tidy(model_ukraine) %>% mutate(Target = "Ukraine"),
  tidy(model_west)    %>% mutate(Target = "West")
) %>%
  filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
  mutate(
    conf.low  = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    Target = factor(Target, levels = c("Russia", "Ukraine", "West"))
  )

# Plot
ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = estimate, y = Target)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.7,
    color = "#2b6cb0"
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "#2b6cb0") +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign expert status and negative sentiment toward geopolitical actors",
    subtitle = "Logistic regression coefficients with 95% confidence intervals, controlling for year",
    caption  = "Right of zero: foreign experts more likely than domestic experts to express negative sentiment.\nLeft of zero: foreign experts less likely than domestic experts.",
    x        = "Foreign expert coefficient (log-odds)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 1.5, by = 0.5),
    labels = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 13, color = "grey30", margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey40", hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 13, color = "grey30", margin = margin(t = 8)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    plot.margin      = margin(12, 16, 12, 12)
  )
```

```{r}
# ── BAR CHART ──────────────────────────────────────────────

ggplot(sentiment_full,
       aes(x = sentiment_label, y = rate, fill = Expert)) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.65,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 3.5,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +

  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 0.068,
           label    = "Positive evaluations",
           size     = 3.5,
           color    = "grey40",
           fontface = "italic") +

  annotate("text", x = 5, y = 0.068,
           label    = "Negative evaluations",
           size     = 3.5,
           color    = "grey40",
           fontface = "italic") +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 0.1),
    limits = c(0, 0.075)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Share of expert mentions",
    title    = "Directed sentiment toward geopolitical actors by expert origin",
    subtitle = "Share of expert mentions expressing positive or negative sentiment toward Russia, the West, and Ukraine"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )


# ── EXPANDED REGRESSION PLOT ────────────────────────────────

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

# Negative sentiment models
model_neg_west    <- glm(anti_west    ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_neg_russia  <- glm(anti_russia  ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_neg_ukraine <- glm(anti_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)

# Positive sentiment models
model_pos_west    <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_pos_russia  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
model_pos_ukraine <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + year,
                         data = master_dataset, family = binomial)

# Extract and combine
coef_df <- bind_rows(
  tidy(model_neg_russia)  %>% mutate(Target = "Russia",  Direction = "Negative"),
  tidy(model_neg_ukraine) %>% mutate(Target = "Ukraine", Direction = "Negative"),
  tidy(model_neg_west)    %>% mutate(Target = "West",    Direction = "Negative"),
  tidy(model_pos_russia)  %>% mutate(Target = "Russia",  Direction = "Positive"),
  tidy(model_pos_ukraine) %>% mutate(Target = "Ukraine", Direction = "Positive"),
  tidy(model_pos_west)    %>% mutate(Target = "West",    Direction = "Positive")
) %>%
  filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
  mutate(
    conf.low  = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    Target    = factor(Target,    levels = c("Russia", "Ukraine", "West")),
    Direction = factor(Direction, levels = c("Positive", "Negative"))
  )

ggplot(coef_df,
       aes(x = estimate, y = Target, color = Direction)) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey40"
  ) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height   = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
  ) +

  geom_point(
    size     = 3,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Positive" = "#4a7fb0", "Negative" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = "Sentiment direction"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = paste0(
      "Effect of foreign expert status (log-odds)\n",
      "Right of zero: foreign experts more likely than domestic experts\n",
      "Left of zero: foreign experts less likely than domestic experts"
    ),
    y = NULL,
    title   = "Effect of foreign expert status on directed sentiment toward geopolitical actors",
    subtitle = "Logistic regression coefficients with 95% confidence intervals, controlling for year.\nBlue = positive sentiment, Red = negative sentiment."
  )+

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )


```

## H3: Foreign expert + topic + sentiment

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggalluvial)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Clean topic names
# -----------------------------
master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = recode(dominant_topic,
      "ukraine_war" = "Ukraine war",
      "international_geopolitics" = "International geopolitics",
      "military_technology" = "Military technology",
      "energy_sanctions" = "Energy and sanctions",
      "macro_economy" = "Macro economy",
      "courts_crime" = "Courts and crime",
      "kremlin_governance" = "Kremlin governance",
      "covid_health" = "Covid and health",
      "information_services" = "Information services",
      "housing_market" = "Housing market",
      "regions_local" = "Regions and local issues",
      "development_education" = "Development and education",
      "media_society" = "Media and society",
      "general_news" = "General news",
      "opinion_discourse" = "Opinion discourse",
      "no_topic" = "No clear topic"
    ),
    Expert_origin = factor(foreign_expert,
                           labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")),
    Sentiment = sentiment_category
  )

# -----------------------------
# 2. FULL PLOT (ALL TOPICS)
# -----------------------------
alluvial_full <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(Sentiment != "Neutral/Other") %>%
  count(dominant_topic_clean, Expert_origin, Sentiment)

p_full <- ggplot(
  alluvial_full,
  aes(
    axis1 = dominant_topic_clean,
    axis2 = Expert_origin,
    axis3 = Sentiment,
    y = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = Sentiment),
    width = 0.18,
    alpha = 0.85,
    knot.pos = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_stratum(width = 0.18, fill = "grey90", color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 2.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Anti-West" = "#2166ac",
      "Anti-Ukraine" = "#67a9cf",
      "Anti-Russia" = "#b2182b"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.05, .05)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Number of mentions",
    title = "How topics, expert origin, and sentiment are connected"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 40, 10, 40)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 3. FOCUSED PLOT (THEORY-RELEVANT TOPICS ONLY)
# -----------------------------
relevant_topics <- c(
  "Ukraine war",
  "International geopolitics",
  "Energy and sanctions",
  "Military technology"
)

alluvial_focus <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    Sentiment != "Neutral/Other",
    dominant_topic_clean %in% relevant_topics
  ) %>%
  count(dominant_topic_clean, Expert_origin, Sentiment)

p_focus <- ggplot(
  alluvial_focus,
  aes(
    axis1 = dominant_topic_clean,
    axis2 = Expert_origin,
    axis3 = Sentiment,
    y = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = Sentiment),
    width = 0.2,
    alpha = 0.9,
    knot.pos = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_stratum(width = 0.2, fill = "grey92", color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Anti-West" = "#2166ac",
      "Anti-Ukraine" = "#67a9cf",
      "Anti-Russia" = "#b2182b"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.08, .08)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Number of mentions",
    title = "Foreign experts in geopolitical coverage are disproportionately associated with anti-West sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 50, 10, 50)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 4. PRINT BOTH
# -----------------------------
p_full
p_focus
```

```{r}


# ============================================================
# SECTION 8.4: H3 — Interaction (topic × foreign expert)
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(broom)
library(scales)
library(ggalluvial)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Ensure dominant_topic_clean exists and is a factor
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = recode(dominant_topic,
      "ukraine_war"              = "Ukraine war",
      "international_geopolitics"= "International geopolitics",
      "military_technology"      = "Military technology",
      "energy_sanctions"         = "Energy and sanctions",
      "macro_economy"            = "Macroeconomy",
      "courts_crime"             = "Courts and crime",
      "kremlin_governance"       = "Kremlin governance",
      "covid_health"             = "COVID-19 and health",
      "information_services"     = "Information services",
      "housing_market"           = "Housing market",
      "regions_local"            = "Regional and local issues",
      "development_education"    = "Development and education",
      "media_society"            = "Media and society",
      "general_news"             = "General news",
      "opinion_discourse"        = "Opinion and commentary",
      "no_topic"                 = "No clear topic"
    )
  )

# Set a neutral domestic topic as reference level
master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean <- relevel(
  factor(master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean),
  ref = "Courts and crime"   # stable domestic baseline
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Fit interaction model
# ------------------------------------------------------------

model_h3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * dominant_topic_clean + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model_h3)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# FIGURE X: Predicted probability plot (topic × foreign expert)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

pred_h3 <- ggpredict(
  model_h3,
  terms = c("dominant_topic_clean", "foreign_expert [0,1]")
)

pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_h3) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(group,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic expert", "Foreign expert")),
    # Calculate gap to sort topics by divergence
    topic = x
  )

# Calculate the gap between foreign and domestic for ordering
topic_gap <- pred_df %>%
  select(topic, Expert, predicted) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Expert, values_from = predicted) %>%
  mutate(gap = `Foreign expert` - `Domestic expert`) %>%
  arrange(gap)

pred_df$topic <- factor(pred_df$topic, levels = topic_gap$topic)

ggplot(pred_df,
       aes(x = topic,
           y = predicted,
           color = Expert,
           shape = Expert)) +

  geom_point(size = 3,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +

  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width = 0.25,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
  ) +

  coord_flip() +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Domestic expert" = "#b04a4a",
               "Foreign expert"  = "#2b6cb0")
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +

  labs(
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment",
    color = NULL,
    shape = NULL,
    title = "Anti-West sentiment across topics by expert origin"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# FIGURE X: Interaction coefficient plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------

coef_h3 <- tidy(model_h3, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("foreign_expert:dominant_topic_clean", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    topic = gsub("foreign_expert:dominant_topic_clean", "", term),
    topic = gsub("_", " ", topic),
    # Tag geopolitical topics
    is_geo = topic %in% c(
      "Ukraine war",
      "International geopolitics",
      "Military technology",
      "Energy and sanctions"
    )
  )

ggplot(coef_h3,
       aes(x = estimate,
           y = reorder(topic, estimate),
           color = is_geo)) +

  geom_point(size = 3) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height = 0.25
  ) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype   = "dashed",
             color      = "grey40") +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE"  = "#2b6cb0",
               "FALSE" = "grey50"),
    labels = c("TRUE"  = "Geopolitical topic",
               "FALSE" = "Domestic topic"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  labs(
    x     = "Interaction effect (foreign expert × topic, log-odds)",
    y     = NULL,
    title = "Interaction between foreign expert status and topic\non anti-West sentiment"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# FIGURE X: Alluvial diagram — geopolitical topics only
# (your existing code, tidied up)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Ensure sentiment_category exists
master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_category = case_when(
      anti_west    == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
      anti_ukraine == 1 ~ "Anti-Ukraine",
      anti_russia  == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
      TRUE              ~ "Neutral/Other"
    ),
    Expert_origin = factor(
      foreign_expert,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")
    )
  )

relevant_topics <- c(
  "Ukraine war",
  "International geopolitics",
  "Energy and sanctions",
  "Military technology"
)

alluvial_focus <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    sentiment_category   != "Neutral/Other",
    dominant_topic_clean %in% relevant_topics
  ) %>%
  count(dominant_topic_clean, Expert_origin, sentiment_category)

ggplot(
  alluvial_focus,
  aes(
    axis1 = dominant_topic_clean,
    axis2 = Expert_origin,
    axis3 = sentiment_category,
    y     = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = sentiment_category),
    width    = 0.2,
    alpha    = 0.9,
    knot.pos = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_stratum(width = 0.2, fill = "grey92", color = "grey40") +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Anti-West"    = "#2166ac",
      "Anti-Ukraine" = "#67a9cf",
      "Anti-Russia"  = "#b2182b"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.08, .08)
  ) +
  labs(
    y     = "Number of mentions",
    fill  = NULL,
    title = "Topic, expert origin, and sentiment in geopolitical coverage",
    subtitle = "Foreign experts in geopolitical topics are disproportionately\nassociated with anti-West evaluations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 50, 10, 50)
  )
```

```{r}

# Simple gap visualization for Mechanism 2
domestic_rates <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(foreign_expert == 0) %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(domestic_rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE))

foreign_rates <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(foreign_expert == 1) %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(foreign_rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE))

gap_data <- left_join(domestic_rates, foreign_rates, 
                      by = "dominant_topic_clean") %>%
  mutate(
    gap = foreign_rate - domestic_rate,
    ratio = foreign_rate / domestic_rate,
    topic_type = ifelse(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ),
      "Geopolitical", "Domestic/other"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(ratio))
gap_data

master_dataset %>%
  filter(anti_west == 1) %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    foreign_share_of_antiwest = mean(foreign_expert),
    n_antiwest = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(foreign_share_of_antiwest))

# Combine share data for the key comparison
master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    foreign_share_of_experts   = mean(foreign_expert),
    foreign_share_of_antiwest  = mean(foreign_expert[anti_west == 1]),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    overrepresentation = foreign_share_of_antiwest / foreign_share_of_experts,
    topic_type = ifelse(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ),
      "Geopolitical", "Domestic/other"
    )
  )
```

