```{r}
# ============================================================
# H3 REVISED PLOTS + REGRESSION
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggalluvial)
library(scales)
library(broom)
library(ggeffects)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# SHARED DATA PREP
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_category = case_when(
      anti_west    == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
      anti_ukraine == 1 ~ "Anti-Ukraine",
      anti_russia  == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
      TRUE              ~ "Neutral/Other"
    ),
    Expert_origin = factor(
      foreign_expert,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")
    ),
    topic_type = ifelse(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war",
        "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology",
        "Energy and sanctions"
      ),
      "Geopolitical", "Domestic/other"
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 1: DUMBBELL CHART
# Share of experts vs share of anti-West mentions per topic
# The gap between dots = overrepresentation
# ============================================================

dumbbell_data <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type) %>%
  summarise(
    share_of_experts  = mean(foreign_expert, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_of_antiwest = sum(foreign_expert[anti_west == 1], na.rm = TRUE) /
                        max(sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    n_antiwest        = sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_antiwest >= 15) %>%
  mutate(gap = share_of_antiwest - share_of_experts) %>%
  arrange(gap)

# Set topic order by gap size
dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(
  dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean,
  levels = dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean
)

# Reshape to long for plotting
dumbbell_long <- dumbbell_data %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(share_of_experts, share_of_antiwest),
    names_to  = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    measure = recode(measure,
      "share_of_experts"  = "Share of all expert mentions",
      "share_of_antiwest" = "Share of anti-West mentions"
    )
  )


# Add this right before the dumbbell plot
dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean <- as.character(dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean)
dumbbell_long$dominant_topic_clean <- as.character(dumbbell_long$dominant_topic_clean)

# Then re-set the factor order
dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(
  dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean,
  levels = dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean[order(dumbbell_data$gap)]
)

dumbbell_long$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(
  dumbbell_long$dominant_topic_clean,
  levels = levels(dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean)
)

fig1 <- ggplot() +

  # Connecting line (the gap)
  geom_segment(
    data = dumbbell_data,
    aes(
      x     = share_of_experts,
      xend  = share_of_antiwest,
      y     = dominant_topic_clean,
      yend  = dominant_topic_clean,
      color = topic_type
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    alpha     = 0.6
  ) +

  # Left dot: share of experts
  geom_point(
    data = dumbbell_long %>% filter(measure == "Share of all expert mentions"),
    aes(x = value, y = dominant_topic_clean),
    color = "grey40",
    size  = 3
  ) +

  # Right dot: share of anti-West mentions
  geom_point(
    data = dumbbell_long %>% filter(measure == "Share of anti-West mentions"),
    aes(x = value, y = dominant_topic_clean, color = topic_type),
    size  = 4
  ) +

  # Reference line at 0 gap
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = NA  # placeholder — visual anchor comes from data
  ) +

  # Annotations explaining dots
  annotate("text", x = 0.22, y = 0.4,
           label = "← Share of\n   all experts",
           size = 3, color = "grey40", hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.60, y = 0.4,
           label = "Share of\nanti-West →",
           size = 3, color = "grey40", hjust = 0) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Geopolitical" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic/other" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = "Share (%)",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "Foreign experts produce anti-West sentiment\nfar beyond their numerical presence",
    subtitle = "Each line connects a topic's foreign expert share (grey dot) to their\nshare of all anti-West mentions (colored dot). Longer lines = greater overrepresentation."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )

fig1

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 2: GROUPED BAR CHART
# Anti-West rate by topic and expert origin
# Clean comparison of domestic vs foreign rate per topic
# ============================================================

bar_data <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type, Expert_origin) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_west_rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    n              = n(),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Order topics by foreign expert anti-West rate
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  mutate(
    foreign_rate = anti_west_rate[Expert_origin == "Foreign"]
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(foreign_rate))

bar_data$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(
  bar_data$dominant_topic_clean,
  levels = unique(bar_data$dominant_topic_clean[bar_data$Expert_origin == "Foreign"])
)

fig2 <- ggplot(
  bar_data,
  aes(
    x    = anti_west_rate,
    y    = dominant_topic_clean,
    fill = Expert_origin
  )
) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.65,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  # Vertical separator between geopolitical and domestic
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 4.5,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey60",
    linewidth  = 0.5
  ) +

  annotate("text", x = 0.155, y = 5.1,
           label = "Geopolitical topics",
           size = 3, color = "grey40", hjust = 1) +

  annotate("text", x = 0.155, y = 3.9,
           label = "Domestic topics",
           size = 3, color = "grey40", hjust = 1) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 0.20)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "Foreign experts are more anti-West in every topic,\nbut the gap is largest in domestic domains",
    subtitle = "Anti-West rate for foreign (blue) and domestic (red) experts by topic"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )

fig2

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3: SIMPLIFIED ALLUVIAL
# 3 domestic topics only, Anti-Russia vs Anti-West only
# Shows: red flows through Domestic, blue flows through Foreign
# ============================================================

alluvial_simple <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    sentiment_category %in% c("Anti-West", "Anti-Russia"),
    dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
      "Macroeconomy",
      "Information services",
      "Courts and crime"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = as.character(dominant_topic_clean),
    Expert_origin        = as.character(Expert_origin),
    sentiment_category   = as.character(sentiment_category)
  ) %>%
  count(dominant_topic_clean, Expert_origin, sentiment_category)

fig3 <- ggplot(
  alluvial_simple,
  aes(
    axis1 = dominant_topic_clean,
    axis2 = Expert_origin,
    axis3 = sentiment_category,
    y     = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = sentiment_category),
    width    = 0.25,
    alpha    = 0.85,
    knot.pos = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_stratum(
    width = 0.25,
    fill  = "grey93",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Anti-West"   = "#2166ac",
      "Anti-Russia" = "#b2182b"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.1, .1)
  ) +
  labs(
    y        = "Number of mentions",
    title    = "In domestic topics, anti-West framing\nflows almost exclusively through foreign experts",
    subtitle = "Anti-Russia sentiment flows through domestic experts;\nanti-West sentiment flows through foreign experts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    axis.text.x      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid       = element_blank()
  )

fig3

# ============================================================
# REGRESSION: Interaction model
# anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_type + year
# topic_type is binary: Geopolitical vs Domestic/other
# This is cleaner than 15-way interaction and directly
# tests the theoretical claim
# ============================================================

# Set reference: Domestic/other topics, domestic experts
master_dataset$topic_type <- relevel(
  factor(master_dataset$topic_type),
  ref = "Domestic/other"
)

model_h3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_type + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model_h3)

# What this model gives you:
# foreign_expert          = effect of being foreign in DOMESTIC topics
# topic_typeGeopolitical  = effect of geopolitical topic for DOMESTIC experts
# foreign_expert:topic_typeGeopolitical = how much the foreign expert
#                           effect CHANGES in geopolitical vs domestic topics
#                           (this is the key interaction term)

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 4: REGRESSION PLOT — Predicted probabilities
# The interaction visualized as marginal effects
# ============================================================

pred_h3 <- ggpredict(
  model_h3,
  terms = c("foreign_expert [0,1]", "topic_type")
)

pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_h3) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(x,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic expert", "Foreign expert")),
    Topic  = factor(group,
                    levels = c("Domestic/other", "Geopolitical"),
                    labels = c("Domestic topics", "Geopolitical topics"))
  )

fig4 <- ggplot(
  pred_df,
  aes(
    x     = Expert,
    y     = predicted,
    color = Topic,
    group = Topic
  )
) +

  # Lines connecting domestic to foreign within each topic type
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1.2,
    alpha     = 0.8
  ) +

  geom_point(size = 4) +

  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width     = 0.08,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Domestic topics"     = "#b04a4a",
      "Geopolitical topics" = "#2b6cb0"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment",
    title    = "The foreign expert effect is larger in domestic\ntopics than in geopolitical ones",
    subtitle = "Steeper slope = larger effect of foreign expert status on anti-West framing.\nIn geopolitical topics, domestic experts are already anti-West."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )

fig4

# ============================================================
# BONUS: Coefficient table for the regression
# Clean summary of what the model says
# ============================================================

tidy(model_h3, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term,
      "foreign_expert"                        = "Foreign expert (in domestic topics)",
      "topic_typeGeopolitical"                = "Geopolitical topic (domestic experts)",
      "year"                                  = "Year trend",
      "foreign_expert:topic_typeGeopolitical" = "Foreign expert × Geopolitical (interaction)"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3)))

# The interaction term is the key result:
# If negative: foreign expert effect is SMALLER in geopolitical topics
#              (consistent with what we found — domestic baseline is already high)
# This supports the reframed H3: foreign experts are most distinctively
# anti-West in domestic topics where domestic experts rarely produce such framing
```

```{r}

# ============================================================
# H3 REVISED PLOTS — FIXED VERSION
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggalluvial)
library(scales)
library(broom)
library(ggeffects)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# SHARED DATA PREP — run this first
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_category = case_when(
      anti_west    == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
      anti_ukraine == 1 ~ "Anti-Ukraine",
      anti_russia  == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
      TRUE              ~ "Neutral/Other"
    ),
    Expert_origin = factor(
      foreign_expert,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")
    ),
    topic_type = ifelse(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war",
        "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology",
        "Energy and sanctions"
      ),
      "Geopolitical", "Domestic/other"
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 1: DUMBBELL CHART — FIXED
# ============================================================

dumbbell_data <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type) %>%
  summarise(
    share_of_experts  = mean(foreign_expert, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_of_antiwest = sum(foreign_expert[anti_west == 1], na.rm = TRUE) /
                        max(sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    n_antiwest        = sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups           = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_antiwest >= 15) %>%
  mutate(
    gap = share_of_antiwest - share_of_experts,
    # Force character to avoid factor conflict with regression below
    dominant_topic_clean = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)
  ) %>%
  arrange(gap)

# Set ordered factor AFTER arranging
topic_order <- dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean

dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean <- factor(
  dumbbell_data$dominant_topic_clean,
  levels = topic_order
)

# Reshape to long
dumbbell_long <- dumbbell_data %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(share_of_experts, share_of_antiwest),
    names_to  = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = factor(dominant_topic_clean, levels = topic_order),
    measure = recode(measure,
      "share_of_experts"  = "Share of all expert mentions",
      "share_of_antiwest" = "Share of anti-West mentions"
    )
  )

fig1 <- ggplot() +

  # Horizontal connecting line — the gap
  geom_segment(
    data = dumbbell_data,
    aes(
      x     = share_of_experts,
      xend  = share_of_antiwest,
      y     = dominant_topic_clean,
      yend  = dominant_topic_clean,
      color = topic_type
    ),
    linewidth = 1.4,
    alpha     = 0.65
  ) +

  # Left dot: share of all experts (grey — same for all)
  geom_point(
    data = dumbbell_long %>%
      filter(measure == "Share of all expert mentions"),
    aes(x = value, y = dominant_topic_clean),
    color = "grey45",
    size  = 3
  ) +

  # Right dot: share of anti-West mentions (colored by topic type)
  geom_point(
    data = dumbbell_long %>%
      filter(measure == "Share of anti-West mentions"),
    aes(x = value, y = dominant_topic_clean, color = topic_type),
    size  = 4
  ) +

  # Legend annotation for dots
  annotate(
    "point", x = 0.88, y = 2.2,
    color = "grey45", size = 3
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.90, y = 2.2,
    label = "Share of experts", size = 3,
    color = "grey45", hjust = 0
  ) +
  annotate(
    "point", x = 0.88, y = 1.3,
    color = "#555555", size = 4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.90, y = 1.3,
    label = "Share of anti-West mentions", size = 3,
    color = "grey45", hjust = 0
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Geopolitical"   = "#2b6cb0",
      "Domestic/other" = "#b04a4a"
    ),
    name = "Topic type"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1.05),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = "Share (%)",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "Foreign experts produce anti-West sentiment\nbeyond their numerical presence in every topic",
    subtitle = "Each line connects a topic's foreign expert share (grey dot) to their share\nof all anti-West mentions (colored dot). Longer line = greater overrepresentation."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

fig1

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 2: GROUPED BAR CHART — FIXED ordering
# Geopolitical topics on top, domestic below, separator between
# ============================================================

bar_data <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type, Expert_origin) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_west_rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    n              = n(),
    .groups        = "drop"
  )

# Order: within each topic type, sort by foreign expert anti-West rate
geo_order <- bar_data %>%
  filter(
    topic_type    == "Geopolitical",
    Expert_origin == "Foreign"
  ) %>%
  arrange(anti_west_rate) %>%
  pull(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  as.character()

dom_order <- bar_data %>%
  filter(
    topic_type    == "Domestic/other",
    Expert_origin == "Foreign"
  ) %>%
  arrange(anti_west_rate) %>%
  pull(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  as.character()

# Final order: domestic first (bottom), then geopolitical (top)
topic_order_bar <- c(dom_order, geo_order)

bar_data <- bar_data %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = factor(
      as.character(dominant_topic_clean),
      levels = topic_order_bar
    )
  )

# Separator position: between domestic and geopolitical
sep_pos <- length(dom_order) + 0.5

fig2 <- ggplot(
  bar_data,
  aes(
    x    = anti_west_rate,
    y    = dominant_topic_clean,
    fill = Expert_origin
  )
) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.65,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  # Separator line between topic types
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = sep_pos,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +

  # Topic type labels
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = 0.185,
    y     = sep_pos + 0.4,
    label = "← Geopolitical topics",
    size  = 3.2, color = "grey40", hjust = 1, fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = 0.185,
    y     = sep_pos - 0.4,
    label = "← Domestic topics",
    size  = 3.2, color = "grey40", hjust = 1, fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 0.20),
    breaks = seq(0, 0.20, by = 0.05)
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "Foreign experts are more anti-West in every topic,\nbut the gap is largest in domestic domains",
    subtitle = "Anti-West rate for foreign (blue) and domestic (red) experts by topic"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

fig2

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3: SIMPLIFIED ALLUVIAL — FIXED margins and labels
# ============================================================

alluvial_simple <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    sentiment_category %in% c("Anti-West", "Anti-Russia"),
    dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
      "Macroeconomy",
      "Information services",
      "Courts and crime"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = as.character(dominant_topic_clean),
    Expert_origin        = as.character(Expert_origin),
    sentiment_category   = as.character(sentiment_category)
  ) %>%
  count(dominant_topic_clean, Expert_origin, sentiment_category)

fig3 <- ggplot(
  alluvial_simple,
  aes(
    axis1 = dominant_topic_clean,
    axis2 = Expert_origin,
    axis3 = sentiment_category,
    y     = n
  )
) +
  geom_alluvium(
    aes(fill = sentiment_category),
    width    = 0.25,
    alpha    = 0.85,
    knot.pos = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_stratum(
    width = 0.25,
    fill  = "grey93",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    stat = "stratum",
    aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Anti-West"   = "#2166ac",
      "Anti-Russia" = "#b2182b"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    limits = c("Topic", "Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
    expand = c(.12, .12)   # more padding so labels don't clip
  ) +
  labs(
    y        = "Number of mentions",
    title    = "In domestic topics, anti-West framing flows\nalmost exclusively through foreign experts",
    subtitle = "Anti-Russia sentiment flows through domestic experts;\nanti-West sentiment flows through foreign experts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    axis.text.x      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 30, 10, 30)  # extra horizontal margin
  )

fig3

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 4: REGRESSION PLOT — no changes needed, runs fine
# Just make sure regression was run first
# ============================================================

master_dataset$topic_type <- relevel(
  factor(master_dataset$topic_type),
  ref = "Domestic/other"
)

model_h3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_type + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pred_h3 <- ggpredict(
  model_h3,
  terms = c("foreign_expert [0,1]", "topic_type")
)

pred_df <- as.data.frame(pred_h3) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(x,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic expert", "Foreign expert")),
    Topic  = factor(group,
                    levels = c("Domestic/other", "Geopolitical"),
                    labels = c("Domestic topics", "Geopolitical topics"))
  )

fig4 <- ggplot(
  pred_df,
  aes(
    x     = Expert,
    y     = predicted,
    color = Topic,
    group = Topic
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4, alpha = 0.85) +

  geom_point(size = 4.5) +

  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width     = 0.08,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +

  # Annotate the slopes directly on the plot
  annotate(
    "text", x = 2.08, y = 0.114,
    label = "~1.5× increase",
    size = 3.2, color = "#2b6cb0", hjust = 0
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 2.08, y = 0.044,
    label = "~4× increase",
    size = 3.2, color = "#b04a4a", hjust = 0
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Domestic topics"     = "#b04a4a",
      "Geopolitical topics" = "#2b6cb0"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 0.16)
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.3, 0.6))) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment",
    title    = "The foreign expert effect is larger in domestic\ntopics than in geopolitical ones",
    subtitle = "In geopolitical topics, domestic experts are already anti-West,\ncompressing the marginal effect of foreign expert status"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    plot.margin      = margin(10, 40, 10, 10)
  )

fig4
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# H3 — FULL INTERACTION COEFFICIENT PLOT
# The formal statistical test of the H3 claim
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
library(scales)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Fit the full interaction model
# Reference category: Courts and crime (clearly domestic,
# substantively meaningful baseline)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean <- relevel(
  factor(master_dataset$dominant_topic_clean),
  ref = "Courts and crime"
)

model_full <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * dominant_topic_clean + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Extract interaction terms only
# These are the coefficients on foreign_expert:topic
# Each one answers: "compared to the foreign expert effect
# in Courts and crime, how much larger/smaller is the
# foreign expert effect in this topic?"
# Negative = smaller effect than baseline (Courts and crime)
# Positive = larger effect than baseline
# ------------------------------------------------------------

coef_interaction <- tidy(model_full, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("foreign_expert:dominant_topic_clean", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean topic names
    topic = gsub("foreign_expert:dominant_topic_clean", "", term),

    # Tag topic type
    topic_type = ifelse(
      topic %in% c(
        "Ukraine war",
        "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology",
        "Energy and sanctions"
      ),
      "Geopolitical", "Domestic/other"
    ),

    # Significance flag
    significant = p.value < 0.05
  )

# Order by estimate so pattern is visually clear
coef_interaction <- coef_interaction %>%
  arrange(estimate) %>%
  mutate(topic = factor(topic, levels = topic))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: Plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------

fig_coef <- ggplot(
  coef_interaction,
  aes(
    x     = estimate,
    y     = topic,
    color = topic_type,
    alpha = significant
  )
) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.7
  ) +

  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
    height    = 0.35,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +

  geom_point(size = 3.5) +

  # Baseline label — moved inside plot, clear position
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 0.05,
    y        = 8,        # adjust up/down if needed
    label    = "Baseline: Courts and crime",
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey50",
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Geopolitical"   = "#2b6cb0",
      "Domestic/other" = "#b04a4a"
    ),
    name = "Topic type"
  ) +

  # Raised from 0.4 to 0.65 for non-significant
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = 0.65, "TRUE" = 1.0),
    guide  = "none"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = paste0(
      "Interaction effect: foreign expert \u00d7 topic (log-odds)\n",
      "Negative = foreign expert effect smaller than in baseline topic"
    ),
    y        = NULL,
    title    = "The foreign expert effect on anti-West sentiment\nis systematically smaller in geopolitical topics",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Interaction coefficients from logistic regression ",
      "(anti_west ~ foreign_expert \u00d7 topic + year).\n",
      "Faded points: p > 0.05. Baseline: Courts and crime ",
      "(foreign expert OR = 3.25, p < 0.001)."
    )
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

fig_coef

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Quick model diagnostics to report in text
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Main effects and interaction summary
tidy(model_full, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term %in% c(
    "foreign_expert",
    "year"
  )) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3)))

# What is the foreign expert baseline effect?
# (i.e. in Courts and crime specifically)
baseline_effect <- tidy(model_full, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
  mutate(
    OR       = exp(estimate),
    OR_low   = exp(conf.low),
    OR_high  = exp(conf.high)
  )

cat("Foreign expert baseline effect (Courts and crime):\n")
cat("  Log-odds:", round(baseline_effect$estimate, 3), "\n")
cat("  Odds ratio:", round(baseline_effect$OR, 2), "\n")
cat("  95% CI:", round(baseline_effect$OR_low, 2),
    "—", round(baseline_effect$OR_high, 2), "\n\n")

# Which interaction terms are significant?
cat("Significant interaction terms (p < 0.05):\n")
coef_interaction %>%
  filter(significant) %>%
  select(topic, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))) %>%
  print()

cat("\nNon-significant interaction terms:\n")
coef_interaction %>%
  filter(!significant) %>%
  select(topic, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))) %>%
  print()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
sentiment_full <- master_dataset %>%
  group_by(foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    pos_russia  = mean(any_positive_russia,  na.rm = TRUE),
    pos_west    = mean(any_positive_west,    na.rm = TRUE),
    pos_ukraine = mean(any_positive_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    neg_russia  = mean(anti_russia,          na.rm = TRUE),
    neg_west    = mean(anti_west,            na.rm = TRUE),
    neg_ukraine = mean(anti_ukraine,         na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -foreign_expert,
    names_to  = "sentiment",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")),
    # Clean display labels — no \n, use spaces
    sentiment_label = recode(sentiment,
      "pos_russia"  = "Positive\nRussia",
      "pos_west"    = "Positive\nWest",
      "pos_ukraine" = "Positive\nUkraine",
      "neg_russia"  = "Negative\nRussia",
      "neg_west"    = "Negative\nWest",
      "neg_ukraine" = "Negative\nUkraine"
    ),
    sentiment_label = factor(sentiment_label, levels = c(
      "Positive\nRussia",
      "Positive\nWest",
      "Positive\nUkraine",
      "Negative\nRussia",
      "Negative\nWest",
      "Negative\nUkraine"
    ))
  )

ggplot(sentiment_full,
       aes(x = sentiment_label, y = rate, fill = Expert)) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.65,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 3.5,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +

  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 0.068,
           label    = "Positive evaluations",
           size     = 3.5,
           color    = "grey40",
           fontface = "italic") +

  annotate("text", x = 5, y = 0.068,
           label    = "Negative evaluations",
           size     = 3.5,
           color    = "grey40",
           fontface = "italic") +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 0.1),
    limits = c(0, 0.075)   # raised to accommodate neg_west foreign at 6%
  ) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Share of expert mentions",
    title    = "Foreign experts dominate evaluations of external actors;\ndomestic experts dominate evaluations of Russia",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Foreign experts produce more positive and negative sentiment ",
      "toward the West and Ukraine.\n",
      "Domestic experts produce more positive and negative sentiment toward Russia."
    )
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )
```

H4: Temporal dynamics

```{r}

model_h4 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * year + dominant_topic_clean,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pred_h4 <- ggpredict(model_h4, terms = c("year", "foreign_expert [0,1]"))
plot(pred_h4)
```

```{r}
# 1. Get the formal interaction coefficient
tidy(model_h4, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term == "foreign_expert:year")

# 2. Test whether topic concentration strengthens over time
# Split into three periods and run H1 model in each
master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(period = case_when(
    year <= 2008 ~ "1999-2008",
    year <= 2016 ~ "2009-2016",
    TRUE         ~ "2017-2024"
  ))

# Run topic concentration model by period
library(purrr)

period_models <- master_dataset %>%
  split(.$period) %>%
  map(~glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
           data = .x, family = binomial))

# Extract predicted probabilities for each period
# and compare the spread between highest and lowest topics
map(period_models, ~range(predict(.x, type = "response")))
```

```{r}
# Check foreign-only trend to support Option B
model_foreign_only <- glm(
  anti_west ~ year + dominant_topic_clean,
  data   = master_dataset %>% filter(foreign_expert == 1),
  family = binomial
)

tidy(model_foreign_only, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term == "year")

# And domestic only for comparison
model_domestic_only <- glm(
  anti_west ~ year + dominant_topic_clean,
  data   = master_dataset %>% filter(foreign_expert == 0),
  family = binomial
)

tidy(model_domestic_only, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term == "year")
```

```{r}
yearly_effects <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(!is.na(anti_west), !is.na(foreign_expert)) %>%
  split(.$year) %>%
  map_dfr(function(df) {
    
    # Stricter thresholds to avoid perfect separation
    n_foreign   <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 1)
    n_domestic  <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 0)
    
    # Need anti-West observations in BOTH groups
    aw_foreign  <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 1], na.rm = TRUE)
    aw_domestic <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 0], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if (n_foreign < 100 | n_domestic < 100) return(NULL)
    if (aw_foreign < 5  | aw_domestic < 5)  return(NULL)
    
    tryCatch({
      m <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
               data = df, family = binomial)
      tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
        filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
        mutate(
          year = unique(df$year),
          n    = nrow(df)
        )
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
  }) %>%
  mutate(significant = p.value < 0.05)

ggplot(yearly_effects,
       aes(x = year, y = estimate)) +

  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey40"
  ) +

  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.15,
    fill  = "#2b6cb0"
  ) +

  geom_line(
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +

  geom_point(
    aes(color = significant),
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE"  = "#2b6cb0",
               "FALSE" = "grey60"),
    labels = c("TRUE"  = "p < 0.05",
               "FALSE" = "p > 0.05"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  # Force sensible y-axis limits
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2002, 2024, by = 3)) +

  labs(
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Effect of foreign expert status (log-odds)",
    title    = "Effect of foreign expert status on anti-West sentiment by year",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Annual logistic regression coefficients with 95% confidence intervals.\n",
      "Coloured points indicate statistical significance at p < 0.05. ",
      "Years with fewer than 100 observations\nor fewer than 5 anti-West ",
      "mentions in either group are excluded."
    )
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}

# Sentiment coverage by year and expert type
master_dataset %>%
  group_by(year, foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total      = n(),
    n_anti_west  = sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_anti_russia = sum(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_any_sentiment = sum(
      anti_west | anti_russia | anti_ukraine | 
      any_positive_west | any_positive_russia | any_positive_ukraine,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    pct_any_sentiment = n_any_sentiment / n_total,
    pct_anti_west     = n_anti_west / n_total,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                         levels = c(0,1),
                         labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"))) %>%
  print(n = 60)

master_dataset %>%
  group_by(year, foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    n_anti_west = sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_total     = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                         levels = c(0,1),
                         labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n_anti_west, color = Expert)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Number of anti-West mentions",
    title    = "Anti-West mentions per year by expert origin",
    subtitle = "Raw counts of expert mentions classified as anti-West"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
# Restrict to 2014 onward where cell counts are meaningful
yearly_effects_restricted <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(anti_west),
    !is.na(foreign_expert),
    year >= 2014
  ) %>%
  split(.$year) %>%
  map_dfr(function(df) {
    
    n_foreign  <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 1)
    n_domestic <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 0)
    aw_foreign  <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 1], na.rm = TRUE)
    aw_domestic <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 0], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if (n_foreign < 100 | n_domestic < 100) return(NULL)
    if (aw_foreign < 10 | aw_domestic < 5)  return(NULL)
    
    tryCatch({
      m <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
               data = df, family = binomial)
      tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
        filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
        mutate(year = unique(df$year), n = nrow(df))
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
  }) %>%
  mutate(significant = p.value < 0.05)

ggplot(yearly_effects_restricted,
       aes(x = year, y = estimate)) +

  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey40"
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2022,
    linetype   = "dotted",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text", x = 2022.2, y = 2.8,
    label    = "Ukraine invasion",
    size     = 3, color = "grey40",
    hjust    = 0, fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.15,
    fill  = "#2b6cb0"
  ) +

  geom_line(
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +

  geom_point(
    aes(color = significant),
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("TRUE"  = "#2b6cb0", "FALSE" = "grey60"),
    labels = c("TRUE"  = "p < 0.05", "FALSE" = "p > 0.05"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 3)) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2014, 2024, by = 2)) +

  labs(
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Foreign expert coefficient (log-odds)",
    title    = "Assosiation between foreign expert status and anti-West sentiment, 2014 to 2024",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Annual logistic regression coefficients with 95% confidence intervals.\n",
      "Analysis restricted to 2014 onward where anti-West cell counts ",
      "support reliable estimation."
    )
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
yearly_effects_grouped <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(anti_west),
    !is.na(foreign_expert),
    !is.na(topic_group),
    year >= 2014,
    topic_group %in% c("geopolitics", "crime", "economy", "politics")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_block = if_else(topic_group == "geopolitics", 
                          "Geopolitical", 
                          "Domestic / other")
  ) %>%
  group_by(topic_block, year) %>%
  group_modify(function(df, keys) {
    
    n_foreign   <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 1)
    n_domestic  <- sum(df$foreign_expert == 0)
    aw_foreign  <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 1], na.rm = TRUE)
    aw_domestic <- sum(df$anti_west[df$foreign_expert == 0], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if (n_foreign < 100 | n_domestic < 100) return(tibble())
    if (aw_foreign < 10 | aw_domestic < 5)  return(tibble())
    
    tryCatch({
      m <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert, data = df, family = binomial)
      tidy(m, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
        filter(term == "foreign_expert") %>%
        mutate(n = nrow(df))
    }, error = function(e) tibble())
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    significant  = p.value < 0.05,
    topic_block  = factor(topic_block, levels = c("Geopolitical", "Domestic / other"))
  )

# colours consistent with thesis palette
group_colors <- c("Geopolitical"    = "#2b6cb0",
                  "Domestic / other" = "#b04a4a")

ggplot(yearly_effects_grouped,
       aes(x = year, y = estimate,
           color = topic_block, fill = topic_block)) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2022,
    linetype   = "dotted",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 2022.15, y = 2.8,
    label    = "Ukraine invasion",
    size     = 3, color = "grey40",
    hjust    = 0, fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.12,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = significant),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = group_colors, name = NULL) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = 19, "FALSE" = 1),
    labels = c("TRUE" = "p < 0.05", "FALSE" = "p > 0.05"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 3)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2014, 2024, by = 2)) +
  labs(
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Effect of foreign expert status (log-odds)",
    title    = "Effect of foreign expert status on anti-West sentiment by topic group, 2014 to 2024",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Annual logistic regression coefficients with 95% confidence intervals.\n",
      "Filled points = p < 0.05. Years excluded where cell counts are too sparse for reliable estimation."
    ),
    caption  = "Right of zero: foreign experts more likely than domestic experts to express anti-West sentiment."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey40", hjust = 0,
                                    margin = margin(t = 10)),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.box       = "horizontal"
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(order = 1),
    fill  = "none",
    shape = guide_legend(order = 2)
  )
```

```{r}

# ── OPTION A: Anti-West only ────────────────────────────────

master_dataset %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month, foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_west = sum(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"))
  ) %>%

  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = anti_west, color = Expert)) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2014-03-01"),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2014-03-01"), y = Inf,
           label = "Editorial change", angle = 90,
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 1.1, size = 3,
           color = "grey40", fontface = "italic") +

  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = as.Date("2022-02-24"), y = Inf,
           label = "Ukraine invasion", angle = 90,
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 1.1, size = 3,
           color = "grey40", fontface = "italic") +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 years", date_labels = "%Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Number of anti-West expert mentions",
    title    = "Anti-West expert mentions over time by expert origin",
    subtitle = "Monthly counts. Dashed lines mark the 2014 editorial change and 2022 Ukraine invasion."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )


# Create shared event annotation data
events <- data.frame(
  xintercept = as.Date(c("2014-03-01", "2022-02-24")),
  label      = c("Editorial change", "Ukraine invasion")
)

master_dataset %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month, foreign_expert) %>%
  summarise(
    anti_west = sum(anti_west,         na.rm = TRUE),
    pos_west  = sum(any_positive_west, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west, pos_west),
    names_to  = "sentiment",
    values_to = "n"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(foreign_expert,
                    levels = c(0, 1),
                    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign")),
    Sentiment = factor(sentiment,
                       levels = c("anti_west", "pos_west"),
                       labels = c("Negative (anti-West)",
                                  "Positive (pro-West)"))
  ) %>%

  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = n, color = Expert)) +

  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +

  # Event lines — only label in top panel
  geom_vline(
    data = function(x) x %>% filter(Sentiment == "Negative (anti-West)"),
    aes(xintercept = as.Date("2014-03-01")),
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = function(x) x %>% filter(Sentiment == "Negative (anti-West)"),
    aes(xintercept = as.Date("2022-02-24")),
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = function(x) x %>% filter(Sentiment == "Positive (pro-West)"),
    aes(xintercept = as.Date("2014-03-01")),
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    data = function(x) x %>% filter(Sentiment == "Positive (pro-West)"),
    aes(xintercept = as.Date("2022-02-24")),
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "grey50",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # Labels only in top panel
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = as.Date("2014-03-01"),
    y        = Inf,
    label    = "Editorial change",
    angle    = 90,
    vjust    = -0.4,
    hjust    = 1.1,
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey40",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
    y        = Inf,
    label    = "Ukraine invasion",
    angle    = 90,
    vjust    = -0.4,
    hjust    = 1.1,
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey40",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  facet_wrap(
    ~ Sentiment,
    ncol   = 1,
    # Fixed y scale so magnitudes are comparable
    scales = "fixed"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +

  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "3 years", date_labels = "%Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +

  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Number of expert mentions",
    title    = "West-directed sentiment in expert mentions over time",
    subtitle = "Monthly counts by expert origin and sentiment direction."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}

library(modelsummary)
library(dplyr)
library(kableExtra)

# ── PREPARE DATA ────────────────────────────────────────────

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    topic_group = relevel(
      factor(topic_group),
      ref = "crime"
    )
  )

# ── FIT MODELS ──────────────────────────────────────────────

m1 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m2 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year + topic_group,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m4 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m5 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group +
              foreign_expert * year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ── COEFFICIENT LABELS ──────────────────────────────────────

keep_coefs <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                          = "Foreign expert",
  "year"                                    = "Year",
  "topic_groupgeopolitics"                  = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "topic_groupeconomy"                      = "Topic: Economy",
  "topic_grouppolitics"                     = "Topic: Politics",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupgeopolitics"   = "Foreign expert × Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupeconomy"       = "Foreign expert × Economy",
  "foreign_expert:topic_grouppolitics"      = "Foreign expert × Politics",
  "foreign_expert:year"                     = "Foreign expert × Year"
)

# ── MODEL LIST ───────────────────────────────────────────────

models <- list(
  "M1"  = m1,
  "M2"  = m2,
  "M3"  = m3,
  "M4"  = m4,
  "M5"  = m5
)

# ── TABLE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  models,

  coef_map     = keep_coefs,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",

  gof_map = list(
    list(raw = "nobs",   clean = "N",          fmt = 0),
    list(raw = "AIC",    clean = "AIC",         fmt = 1),
    list(raw = "logLik", clean = "Log-lik.",    fmt = 1)
  ),

  title = paste0(
    "Logistic regression models predicting anti-West ",
    "sentiment in expert mentions (odds ratios)"
  ),

  notes = list(
    paste0(
      "Odds ratios reported. Standard errors in parentheses. ",
      "Baseline category: domestic expert, crime topic."
    ),
    paste0(
      "Topic group aggregates 15 LDA topics into four categories: ",
      "geopolitical (Ukraine war, international geopolitics, ",
      "military technology, energy and sanctions), ",
      "economy (macroeconomy, housing market), ",
      "politics (Kremlin governance, opinion discourse, media and society), ",
      "and crime (courts and crime, baseline)."
    ),
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),

  output = "kableExtra"
)
```

```{r}

# ============================================================
# REGRESSION TABLE — FULL SCRIPT
# Run this entirely in RStudio after loading master_dataset
# ============================================================

library(modelsummary)
library(dplyr)
library(officer)
library(flextable)

# ── PREPARE DATA ────────────────────────────────────────────

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    topic_group = relevel(
      factor(topic_group),
      ref = "crime"
    ),
    # Scale year for numerical stability in M5
    year_scaled = as.numeric(scale(year))
  )

# ── FIT MODELS ──────────────────────────────────────────────

m1 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m2 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year + topic_group,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m4 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

m5 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group +
              foreign_expert * year_scaled,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ── COEFFICIENT LABELS ──────────────────────────────────────

keep_coefs <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                        = "Foreign expert",
  "year"                                  = "Year",
  "year_scaled"                           = "Year (standardized)",
  "topic_groupgeopolitics"                = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "topic_groupeconomy"                    = "Topic: Economy",
  "topic_grouppolitics"                   = "Topic: Politics",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupgeopolitics" = "Foreign expert \u00d7 Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupeconomy"     = "Foreign expert \u00d7 Economy",
  "foreign_expert:topic_grouppolitics"    = "Foreign expert \u00d7 Politics",
  "foreign_expert:year_scaled"            = "Foreign expert \u00d7 Year"
)

# ── MODEL LIST ───────────────────────────────────────────────

models <- list(
  "M1"  = m1,
  "M2"  = m2,
  "M3"  = m3,
  "M4"  = m4,
  "M5"  = m5
)

# ── GOODNESS OF FIT ROWS ─────────────────────────────────────

gof <- list(
  list(raw = "nobs",   clean = "N",          fmt = 0),
  list(raw = "AIC",    clean = "AIC",         fmt = 1),
  list(raw = "logLik", clean = "Log-likelihood", fmt = 1)
)

# ── NOTES ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

table_notes <- paste0(
  "Odds ratios reported with standard errors in parentheses. ",
  "Baseline category: domestic expert in courts and crime coverage. ",
  "Topic group aggregates 15 LDA topics into four categories: ",
  "geopolitical (Ukraine war, international geopolitics, military ",
  "technology, energy and sanctions), economy (macroeconomy, housing ",
  "market), politics (Kremlin governance, opinion discourse, media and ",
  "society), and crime (baseline). Year is standardized in M5 to avoid ",
  "numerical overflow in the interaction term. ",
  "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
)

# ── BUILD TABLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────

tab <- modelsummary(
  models,
  coef_map     = keep_coefs,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = 3,
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Table X. Logistic regression models predicting anti-West sentiment in expert mentions",
  notes        = table_notes,
  output       = "flextable"
)

# ── FORMAT THE FLEXTABLE ─────────────────────────────────────

tab <- tab %>%

  # Font throughout
  font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") %>%

  # Header formatting
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "header") %>%
  align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "body") %>%
  align(j = 2:6, align = "center", part = "body") %>%

  # Borders — clean academic style
  border_remove() %>%
  hline_top(part = "header",
            border = fp_border(width = 1.5)) %>%
  hline(i = 1, part = "header",
        border = fp_border(width = 0.75)) %>%
  hline_bottom(part = "body",
               border = fp_border(width = 1.5)) %>%

  # Group separator lines — visual breaks between sections
  # After main effects (before topic effects)
  hline(i = 2, part = "body",
        border = fp_border(width = 0.5, style = "dashed")) %>%
  # After topic main effects (before interactions)
  hline(i = 8, part = "body",
        border = fp_border(width = 0.5, style = "dashed")) %>%
  # After interactions (before GOF)
  hline(i = 14, part = "body",
        border = fp_border(width = 0.75)) %>%

  # Column widths
  width(j = 1, width = 2.4) %>%
  width(j = 2:6, width = 1.1) %>%

  # Shade alternating coefficient rows lightly for readability
  bg(i = c(1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13),
     bg = "#F7F7F7", part = "body") %>%

  # Table width
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit")

# ── SAVE TO WORD ─────────────────────────────────────────────

doc <- read_docx() %>%
  body_add_par(
    "Table X. Logistic regression models predicting anti-West sentiment",
    style = "heading 3"
  ) %>%
  body_add_flextable(tab) %>%
  body_add_par(table_notes, style = "Normal")

print(doc, target = "regression_table.docx")

cat("Table saved to:", file.path(getwd(), "regression_table.docx"), "\n")


# Clean viewer-friendly version
modelsummary(
  models,
  coef_map     = keep_coefs,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = 3,
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Logistic regression models predicting anti-West sentiment (odds ratios)",
  notes        = table_notes,
  output       = "default"   # renders in RStudio viewer pane
)
```

```{r}
library(modelsummary)
library(dplyr)

# ── PREPARE DATA ────────────────────────────────────────────

master_dataset <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    topic_group = relevel(factor(topic_group), ref = "crime"),
    year_scaled = as.numeric(scale(year))
  )

# ── PANEL A MODELS ───────────────────────────────────────────

pa_m1 <- glm(
  foreign_expert ~ topic_group,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pa_m2 <- glm(
  foreign_expert ~ topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pa_m3 <- glm(
  foreign_expert ~ topic_group * year_scaled,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ── PANEL B MODELS ───────────────────────────────────────────

pb_anti_west <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pb_anti_ukraine <- glm(
  anti_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pb_anti_russia <- glm(
  anti_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pb_pos_west <- glm(
  any_positive_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

pb_pos_russia <- glm(
  any_positive_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ── TABLE 2 MODELS ───────────────────────────────────────────

t2_m1 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

t2_m2 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

t2_m3 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year + topic_group,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

t2_m4 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group + year,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

t2_m5 <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group +
              foreign_expert * year_scaled,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

t2_m3b <- glm(
  anti_russia ~ foreign_expert + year + topic_group,
  data   = master_dataset,
  family = binomial
)

# ── COEFFICIENT LABELS ──────────────────────────────────────

keep_panel_a <- c(
  "topic_groupgeopolitics"             = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "topic_groupeconomy"                 = "Topic: Economy",
  "topic_grouppolitics"                = "Topic: Politics",
  "year"                               = "Year",
  "year_scaled"                        = "Year (standardized)",
  "topic_groupgeopolitics:year_scaled" = "Geopolitical x Year",
  "topic_groupeconomy:year_scaled"     = "Economy x Year",
  "topic_grouppolitics:year_scaled"    = "Politics x Year"
)

keep_panel_b <- c(
  "foreign_expert"         = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_groupgeopolitics" = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "topic_groupeconomy"     = "Topic: Economy",
  "topic_grouppolitics"    = "Topic: Politics",
  "year"                   = "Year"
)

keep_t2 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                        = "Foreign expert",
  "year"                                  = "Year",
  "year_scaled"                           = "Year (standardized)",
  "topic_groupgeopolitics"                = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "topic_groupeconomy"                    = "Topic: Economy",
  "topic_grouppolitics"                   = "Topic: Politics",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupgeopolitics" = "Foreign expert x Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_groupeconomy"     = "Foreign expert x Economy",
  "foreign_expert:topic_grouppolitics"    = "Foreign expert x Politics",
  "foreign_expert:year_scaled"            = "Foreign expert x Year"
)

# ── GOF ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

gof <- list(
  list(raw = "nobs",   clean = "N",             fmt = 0),
  list(raw = "logLik", clean = "Log-likelihood", fmt = 1)
)

gof_t2 <- list(
  list(raw = "nobs",   clean = "N",             fmt = 0),
  list(raw = "AIC",    clean = "AIC",            fmt = 1),
  list(raw = "logLik", clean = "Log-likelihood", fmt = 1)
)

# ── VIEW TABLE 1 PANEL A ─────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n===== TABLE 1 — PANEL A: Topic predicts foreign expert (H1, H1b) =====\n")

modelsummary(
  list(
    "H1/H1b (i)"   = pa_m1,
    "H1/H1b (ii)"  = pa_m2,
    "H1/H1b (iii)" = pa_m3
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_a,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = 3,
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel A: Dependent variable = Foreign expert presence (H1, H1b)",
  notes        = "Odds ratios. Baseline: crime topic. * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001",
  output       = "default"
)

# ── VIEW TABLE 1 PANEL B ─────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n===== TABLE 1 — PANEL B: Foreign expert predicts sentiment (H2a, H2b) =====\n")

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = pb_anti_west,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = pb_anti_ukraine,
    "Anti-Russia"  = pb_anti_russia,
    "Pro-West"     = pb_pos_west,
    "Pro-Russia"   = pb_pos_russia
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_b,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = 3,
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel B: Dependent variable = Sentiment indicators (H2a, H2b)",
  notes        = "Odds ratios. Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001",
  output       = "default"
)

# ── VIEW TABLE 2 ─────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n===== TABLE 2: Framing mechanism (H2a, H2b, H3, H4) =====\n")

modelsummary(
  list(
    "M1: Baseline" = t2_m1,
    "M2: + Year"   = t2_m2,
    "M3: + Topic"  = t2_m3,
    "M4: H3"       = t2_m4,
    "M5: H4"       = t2_m5,
    "M3b: H2b"     = t2_m3b
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_t2,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = 3,
  gof_map      = gof_t2,
  title        = "Table 2: Dependent variable = Anti-West sentiment (M1-M5) and Anti-Russia (M3b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios. Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. ",
    "M3b switches DV to anti-Russia to confirm H2b. ",
    "Year standardized in M5. * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001"
  ),
  output       = "default"
)
```

```{r}
# ── FIX: force fixed decimal format throughout ───────────────

# Rerun Panel A with fixed format
modelsummary(
  list(
    "H1/H1b (i)"   = pa_m1,
    "H1/H1b (ii)"  = pa_m2,
    "H1/H1b (iii)" = pa_m3
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_a,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel A: Dependent variable = Foreign expert presence (H1, H1b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: crime topic. ",
    "Year standardized in model (iii) for numerical stability. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "default"
)

# Rerun Panel B with fixed format
modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = pb_anti_west,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = pb_anti_ukraine,
    "Anti-Russia"  = pb_anti_russia,
    "Pro-West"     = pb_pos_west,
    "Pro-Russia"   = pb_pos_russia
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_b,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel B: Dependent variable = Sentiment indicators (H2a, H2b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "default"
)

# Rerun Table 2 with fixed format and clarified year note
modelsummary(
  list(
    "M1: Baseline" = t2_m1,
    "M2: + Year"   = t2_m2,
    "M3: + Topic"  = t2_m3,
    "M4: H3"       = t2_m4,
    "M5: H4"       = t2_m5,
    "M3b: H2b"     = t2_m3b
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_t2,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof_t2,
  title        = "Table 2: Dependent variable = Anti-West sentiment (M1-M5) and Anti-Russia (M3b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. ",
    "M3b switches the dependent variable to anti-Russia sentiment ",
    "to confirm H2b alongside M3. ",
    "Year is unstandardized in M2 through M4 and standardized in M5 ",
    "to avoid numerical overflow in the foreign expert x year interaction; ",
    "the year coefficients in M2-M4 and M5 are therefore not directly comparable. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "default"
)
```

```{r}
# Table 1 Panel A
modelsummary(
  list(
    "H1/H1b (i)"   = pa_m1,
    "H1/H1b (ii)"  = pa_m2,
    "H1/H1b (iii)" = pa_m3
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_a,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel A: Dependent variable = Foreign expert presence (H1, H1b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: crime topic. ",
    "Year standardized in model (iii) for numerical stability. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "table1a.tex"
)

# Table 1 Panel B
modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = pb_anti_west,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = pb_anti_ukraine,
    "Anti-Russia"  = pb_anti_russia,
    "Pro-West"     = pb_pos_west,
    "Pro-Russia"   = pb_pos_russia
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_panel_b,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof,
  title        = "Panel B: Dependent variable = Sentiment indicators (H2a, H2b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "table1b.tex"
)

# Table 2
modelsummary(
  list(
    "M1: Baseline" = t2_m1,
    "M2: + Year"   = t2_m2,
    "M3: + Topic"  = t2_m3,
    "M4: H3"       = t2_m4,
    "M5: H4"       = t2_m5,
    "M3b: H2b"     = t2_m3b
  ),
  coef_map     = keep_t2,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  stars        = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic    = "({std.error})",
  fmt          = "%.3f",
  gof_map      = gof_t2,
  title        = "Table 2: Dependent variable = Anti-West sentiment (M1--M5) and Anti-Russia (M3b)",
  notes        = paste0(
    "Odds ratios with standard errors in parentheses. ",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. ",
    "M3b switches the dependent variable to anti-Russia sentiment ",
    "to confirm H2b alongside M3. ",
    "Year is unstandardized in M2 through M4 and standardized in M5 ",
    "to avoid numerical overflow in the foreign expert x year interaction; ",
    "the year coefficients in M2--M4 and M5 are therefore not directly comparable. ",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001."
  ),
  output       = "table2.tex"
)
```

# NEW PLOTS and hypotheses: 

## H1: Core selection + evaluation 

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# --- Data preparation ---

# Per-topic stats: foreign expert share AND anti-West rate
topic_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total          = n(),
    n_foreign        = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic       = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    foreign_share    = n_foreign / n_total,  # dot SIZE: how dominated by foreign
    anti_west_foreign   = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1], na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0], na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean, anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Overall foreign expert anti-West rate (for reference line) ---
overall_foreign_antiwest <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(foreign_expert == 1,
         dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(rate)

# --- Color scheme ---
topic_colors <- c(
  "Geopolitical"     = "#2b6cb0",
  "Domestic / other" = "#718096"
)

# --- Plot ---
p1 <- ggplot(topic_stats, 
             aes(x = anti_west_foreign, 
                 y = dominant_topic_clean)) +

  # Dotted connector between domestic tick and foreign dot
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x    = anti_west_domestic,
      xend = anti_west_foreign,
      yend = dominant_topic_clean
    ),
    color     = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.4,
    linetype  = "dotted"
  ) +

  # Domestic expert rate — red tick for reference
  geom_point(
    aes(x = anti_west_domestic),
    shape = 124,
    size  = 4,
    color = "#b04a4a",
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +

  # Foreign expert rate — primary story
  # Sized by foreign share of topic: big = foreign dominated, small = rare
  geom_point(
    aes(size  = foreign_share,
        color = topic_type),
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +

  # Overall foreign expert anti-West reference line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = overall_foreign_antiwest,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth  = 0.5,
    alpha      = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = overall_foreign_antiwest + 0.001,
    y        = 1.5,
    label    = "Avg. foreign\nexpert rate",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3,
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Scales
  scale_color_manual(
    values = topic_colors,
    name   = "Topic type"
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "Foreign experts\nas share of topic",
    range  = c(2, 12),
    breaks = c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75),
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.08))
  ) +

  # Labels
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign experts are anti-West across all topics,\nand dominate the expert pool where it matters most",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Dot = foreign expert anti-West rate per topic. ",
      "Dot size = share of all expert mentions that are foreign.\n",
      "Red tick | = domestic expert anti-West rate for reference."
    ),
    x       = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y       = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999–2024."
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10, 
                                      lineheight = 1.3),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position    = "right",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8)
  )

p1


```

```{r}

plot_data_long <- topic_stats %>%
  select(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type, 
         anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic, foreign_share) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign", 
                           "Foreign", "Domestic"),
    # Label showing foreign share, only on the foreign bar
    share_label  = if_else(
      expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
      paste0(round(foreign_share * 100), "% of topic"),
      NA_character_
    )
  )

p_bars <- ggplot(plot_data_long,
                 aes(x    = anti_west_rate,
                     y    = dominant_topic_clean,
                     fill = expert_label)) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.6,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  # Foreign share label at end of foreign bar
  geom_text(
    aes(label = share_label),
    position  = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    hjust     = -0.1,
    size      = 2.8,
    color     = "grey40",
    na.rm     = TRUE
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign experts are more anti-West in every topic,\nand dominate the expert pool in geopolitical ones",
    subtitle = "Grey percentage = share of all expert mentions in that topic that are foreign.",
    x        = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999–2024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
    legend.position    = "top",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

p_bars
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# --- Data preparation ---

topic_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total        = n(),
    n_foreign      = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic     = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    foreign_share  = n_foreign / n_total,
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1], 
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0], 
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    # Both panels ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate descending
    # This is the key fix: ordering is set once here and inherited by both panels
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean, 
                                       anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Color scheme ---
topic_colors <- c(
  "Geopolitical"     = "#2b6cb0",
  "Domestic / other" = "#718096"
)

# --- Left panel: Selection ---
# Bar length = foreign share of topic expert pool
# Color = topic type (geopolitical vs domestic)

p_left <- ggplot(topic_stats,
                 aes(x    = foreign_share,
                     y    = dominant_topic_clean,
                     fill = topic_type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +

  # Percentage labels at end of bars
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(foreign_share * 100), "%")),
    hjust  = -0.15,
    size   = 3,
    color  = "grey40"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = topic_colors,
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12)),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Selection",
    subtitle = "Foreign experts as share\nof all expert mentions in topic",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, 
                                    color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9, 
                                    lineheight = 1.3),
```
