```{r}
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

# --- Right panel: Evaluation ---
# Connected dot plot: foreign (blue circle) vs domestic (red square)
# Both ordered by the same factor as left panel

# Pivot to long format for the connected dot plot
right_data <- topic_stats %>%
  select(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type,
         anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

# Find x position for the directional annotation
# Place it in the top portion of the panel where there is whitespace
# International geopolitics will be at the top (highest anti_west_foreign)
# so we annotate in the lower portion to avoid overlap
annotation_y_topic <- levels(topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean)[3]

p_right <- ggplot(right_data,
                  aes(x     = anti_west_rate,
                      y     = dominant_topic_clean,
                      color = expert_label,
                      shape = expert_label)) +

  # Connector line between domestic and foreign dots
  geom_line(
    aes(group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +

  # Points
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +

  # Directional annotation showing foreign is always higher
  # Arrow points right to indicate foreign > domestic in every topic
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x         = 0.005,
    xend      = 0.02,
    y         = annotation_y_topic,
    yend      = annotation_y_topic,
    arrow     = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 0.021,
    y        = annotation_y_topic,
    label    = "Foreign always\nhigher",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Media and society footnote marker
  # This topic has high foreign share but low anti-West rate
  # Flag it so readers notice the anomaly
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = topic_stats$anti_west_foreign[
                 topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean == "Media and society"
               ] + 0.003,
    y        = "Media and society",
    label    = "*",
    color    = "grey40",
    size     = 5,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = 16, "Domestic" = 15),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.12)),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Evaluation",
    subtitle = "Anti-West sentiment rate\nby expert origin",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, 
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9, 
                                      lineheight = 1.3),
    # Remove y-axis text from right panel since left panel carries it
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

# --- Combine panels with patchwork ---

p_combined <- p_left + p_right +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title   = "Foreign experts dominate geopolitical topics and are\nconsistently more anti-West wherever they appear",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Left: share of expert mentions in each topic attributed to foreign experts. ",
      "Right: anti-West sentiment rate by expert origin.\n",
      "* Media and society has high foreign share but low anti-West rate, ",
      "consistent with descriptive rather than evaluative foreign commentary in that topic."
    ),
    caption = paste0(
      "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024. ",
      "Topics ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate (descending)."
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, 
                                   lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9, 
                                   lineheight = 1.4),
      plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8)
    )
  )

p_combined

# --- Save ---
ggsave(
  "figure_selection_evaluation.png",
  plot   = p_combined,
  width  = 12,
  height = 7,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# --- Data preparation ---

topic_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total            = n(),
    n_foreign          = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic         = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    foreign_share      = n_foreign / n_total,
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean,
                                       anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Separator position ---

topic_levels    <- levels(topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean)
geo_topics      <- topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean[
                     topic_stats$topic_type == "Geopolitical"
                   ]
domestic_topics <- topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean[
                     topic_stats$topic_type == "Domestic / other"
                   ]

geo_positions   <- which(topic_levels %in% as.character(geo_topics))
dom_positions   <- which(topic_levels %in% as.character(domestic_topics))

lowest_geo_pos  <- min(geo_positions)
separator_y     <- lowest_geo_pos - 0.5

# Section labels: just above and just below the separator line
geo_label_y <- separator_y + 0.45   # just above line = bottom of geo block
dom_label_y <- separator_y - 0.45   # just below line = top of domestic block

# --- Aggregate summary rows ---

summary_rows <- topic_stats %>%
  group_by(topic_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total            = sum(n_total),
    n_foreign          = sum(n_foreign),
    n_domestic         = sum(n_domestic),
    foreign_share      = n_foreign / n_total,
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west_foreign * n_foreign,
                             na.rm = TRUE) / sum(n_foreign),
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west_domestic * n_domestic,
                             na.rm = TRUE) / sum(n_domestic),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = if_else(
      topic_type == "Geopolitical",
      "Geopolitical\n(combined)",
      "Domestic / other\n(combined)"
    ),
    is_summary = TRUE
  )

# --- Left panel data (individual topics) ---

left_data <- topic_stats %>%
  mutate(is_summary = FALSE) %>%
  select(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type, is_summary,
         n_foreign, n_domestic, n_total, foreign_share) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(n_foreign, n_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "n_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

pct_labels <- topic_stats %>%
  mutate(
    is_summary = FALSE,
    label      = paste0(round(foreign_share * 100), "% foreign")
  ) %>%
  select(dominant_topic_clean, n_total, foreign_share, is_summary, label)

# --- Right panel data (individual topics) ---

right_data <- topic_stats %>%
  select(dominant_topic_clean, topic_type,
         anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

annotation_topic <- "COVID-19 and health"

# x position for section labels: just to the left of the right edge
# so they sit right-aligned against the separator area
label_x <- max(topic_stats$n_total) * 0.98

# --- Main left panel ---

p_left_main <- ggplot(left_data,
                      aes(x    = count,
                          y    = dominant_topic_clean,
                          fill = expert_label)) +
  geom_col(
    position = "stack",
    width    = 0.6,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data        = pct_labels,
    aes(x     = n_total,
        y     = dominant_topic_clean,
        label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust       = -0.1,
    size        = 2.9,
    color       = "grey35"
  ) +

  geom_hline(
    yintercept = separator_y,
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.6,
    linetype   = "solid"
  ) +

  # "Geopolitical" label: right-aligned, just above the separator line
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = label_x,
    y        = geo_label_y,
    label    = "Geopolitical \u25B2",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  # "Domestic / other" label: right-aligned, just below the separator line
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = label_x,
    y        = dom_label_y,
    label    = "Domestic / other \u25BC",
    color    = "#718096",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = comma_format(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.30))
  ) +
  labs(
    # Title and subtitle moved to summary panel
    # so left of main panel has no title
    title    = "Selection",
    subtitle = "Number of expert mentions\nby origin (foreign / domestic)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12,
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9,
                                      lineheight = 1.3),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 9.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

# --- Main right panel ---

p_right_main <- ggplot(right_data,
                       aes(x     = anti_west_rate,
                           y     = dominant_topic_clean,
                           color = expert_label,
                           shape = expert_label)) +
  geom_line(
    aes(group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +

  geom_hline(
    yintercept = separator_y,
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.6,
    linetype   = "solid"
  ) +

  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +

  annotate(
    "segment",
    x         = 0.004,
    xend      = 0.016,
    y         = annotation_topic,
    yend      = annotation_topic,
    arrow     = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 0.017,
    y        = annotation_topic,
    label    = "Foreign always\nhigher",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  annotate(
    "text",
    x = topic_stats$anti_west_foreign[
          topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean == "Media and society"
        ] + 0.002,
    y        = "Media and society",
    label    = "*",
    color    = "grey40",
    size     = 5,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = 16, "Domestic" = 15),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15)),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Evaluation",
    subtitle = "Anti-West sentiment rate\nby expert origin",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12,
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9,
                                      lineheight = 1.3),
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

# --- Summary left panel ---
# Title "Selection" lives here so it sits above the aggregate row

summary_left_data <- summary_rows %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(n_foreign, n_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "n_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

summary_pct <- summary_rows %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(round(foreign_share * 100), "% foreign"))

p_sum_left <- ggplot(summary_left_data,
                     aes(x    = count,
                         y    = dominant_topic_clean,
                         fill = expert_label)) +
  geom_col(
    position = "stack",
    width    = 0.5,
    alpha    = 0.95
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data        = summary_pct,
    aes(x     = n_total,
        y     = dominant_topic_clean,
        label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust       = -0.1,
    size        = 3.5,
    fontface    = "bold",
    color       = "grey25"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = comma_format(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.28))
  ) +
  labs(
    # Selection heading lives here, above the aggregate bars
    title    = "Selection",
    subtitle = "Aggregated counts by topic group",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12,
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1,
                                      face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.margin        = margin(b = 4)
  )

# --- Summary right panel ---
# Title "Evaluation" lives here

summary_right_data <- summary_rows %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

p_sum_right <- ggplot(summary_right_data,
                      aes(x     = anti_west_rate,
                          y     = dominant_topic_clean,
                          color = expert_label,
                          shape = expert_label)) +
  geom_line(
    aes(group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.95) +

  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(anti_west_rate * 100, 1), "%")),
    vjust    = -1.1,
    size     = 3,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = 16, "Domestic" = 15),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15)),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    # Evaluation heading lives here
    title    = "Evaluation",
    subtitle = "Aggregated anti-West rate by topic group",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12,
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9),
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.margin        = margin(b = 4)
  )

# --- Remove titles from main panels since headings live in summary row ---

p_left_main  <- p_left_main  + labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL)
p_right_main <- p_right_main + labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL)

# --- Assemble ---

p_summary_row <- p_sum_left + p_sum_right +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.1, 1))

p_main_row <- p_left_main + p_right_main +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.1, 1))

p_final <- p_summary_row / p_main_row +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 5)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Foreign experts dominate geopolitical topics and are\nconsistently more anti-West wherever they appear",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Top row: aggregated totals for each topic group. ",
      "Bottom: individual topics ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate.\n",
      "Left panels: absolute expert mention counts (stacked). ",
      "Right panels: anti-West sentiment rate by expert origin. ",
      "* Media and society: high foreign share but low anti-West rate."
    ),
    caption  = paste0(
      "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024. ",
      "Topics ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate (descending)."
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                   lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9,
                                   lineheight = 1.4),
      plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8)
    )
  )

p_final

# --- Save ---
ggsave(
  "figure_selection_evaluation_final.png",
  plot   = p_final,
  width  = 13,
  height = 10,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Data ---

scatter_data <- topic_stats %>%
  mutate(
    # Flag outliers for special treatment
    is_outlier = dominant_topic_clean %in% 
                   c("Media and society", "General news"),
    label_text = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)
  )

# --- Plot ---

p_scatter <- ggplot(scatter_data,
                    aes(x     = foreign_share,
                        y     = anti_west_foreign,
                        color = topic_type,
                        label = label_text)) +

  # Trend line without CI — cleaner and less distorted by outlier
  geom_smooth(
    method    = "lm",
    se        = FALSE,
    color     = "grey60",
    linewidth = 0.9,
    aes(group = 1)
  ) +

  # Points — outliers slightly faded
  geom_point(
    aes(alpha = is_outlier),
    size = 3.5
  ) +

  # Labels for normal points
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = scatter_data %>% filter(!is_outlier),
    size          = 3,
    color         = "grey25",
    box.padding   = 0.5,
    point.padding = 0.4,
    segment.color = "grey70",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 20,
    min.segment.length = 0.2
  ) +

  # Outlier labels with italic style and asterisk to flag them
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = scatter_data %>% filter(is_outlier),
    aes(label     = paste0(label_text, "*")),
    size          = 3,
    color         = "grey50",
    fontface      = "italic",
    box.padding   = 0.5,
    point.padding = 0.4,
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 20
  ) +

  # Reference lines at overall averages
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = mean(scatter_data$anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE),
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey75",
    linewidth  = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = mean(scatter_data$foreign_share, na.rm = TRUE),
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey75",
    linewidth  = 0.4
  ) +

  # Quadrant annotation: top right is the "double dominance" zone
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = max(scatter_data$foreign_share) * 0.97,
    y        = max(scatter_data$anti_west_foreign) * 0.97,
    label    = "High foreign share\n+ high anti-West rate",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Geopolitical"     = "#2b6cb0",
               "Domestic / other" = "#718096"),
    name   = "Topic type"
  ) +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = 1, "TRUE" = 0.45),
    guide  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    name   = "Foreign experts as share of topic expert pool"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    name   = "Foreign expert anti-West sentiment rate",
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Topics where foreign experts dominate also show\nthe highest anti-West rates among foreign experts",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Each point is one topic. Trend line (no CI) shows the positive association.\n",
      "Dashed lines = corpus averages. ",
      "* Media and society and General news are exceptions: ",
      "high foreign share but low anti-West rate,\n",
      "consistent with descriptive rather than evaluative foreign commentary in those topics."
    ),
    caption  = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                    lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9,
                                    lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption     = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    legend.position  = "top",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey93")
  )

p_scatter

ggsave(
  "figure_scatter_dominance_antiwest.png",
  plot   = p_scatter,
  width  = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Prepare slope data ---

slope_data <- era_topic_stats %>%
  mutate(
    era_label = recode(as.character(era),
      "1. Pre-2014"   = "Pre-2014",
      "2. 2014-2017"  = "2014\u20132017",
      "3. 2018-2021"  = "2018\u20132021",
      "4. 2022-2024"  = "2022\u20132024"
    ),
    era_label = factor(era_label, levels = c(
      "Pre-2014", "2014\u20132017", "2018\u20132021", "2022\u20132024"
    ))
  )

# Geopolitical topics: individual lines
geo_slope <- slope_data %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Geopolitical", !is_outlier)

# Domestic topics: compute median, q25, q75 band per era
dom_band <- slope_data %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Domestic / other", !is_outlier) %>%
  group_by(era_label) %>%
  summarise(
    median_rate = median(anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25         = quantile(anti_west_foreign, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75         = quantile(anti_west_foreign, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

# Labels: left side (pre-2014) and right side (2022-2024) for geo only
label_left_geo <- geo_slope %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  filter(era_num == min(era_num)) %>%
  ungroup()

label_right_geo <- geo_slope %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  filter(era_num == max(era_num)) %>%
  ungroup()

# --- Plot ---

p_slope_clean <- ggplot() +

  # Domestic band: shaded range
  geom_ribbon(
    data = dom_band,
    aes(x    = era_label,
        ymin = q25,
        ymax = q75,
        group = 1),
    fill  = "#aab8c2",
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +

  # Domestic median line
  geom_line(
    data      = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate, group = 1),
    color     = "#aab8c2",
    linewidth = 1,
    linetype  = "dashed"
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data  = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate),
    color = "#aab8c2",
    size  = 2.5
  ) +

  # Domestic band label — right side only
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 4.18,
    y        = dom_band$median_rate[dom_band$era_label == "2022\u20132024"],
    label    = "Domestic / other\n(median \u00B1 IQR)",
    color    = "#8a9ba8",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # 2014 vertical marker — clean single line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1.5,
    color      = "#b04a4a",
    linewidth  = 0.7,
    linetype   = "solid",
    alpha      = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 1.53,
    y        = max(geo_slope$anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.97,
    label    = "2014 editorial\ntransformation",
    color    = "#b04a4a",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Geopolitical lines — individual, thick, blue
  geom_line(
    data = geo_slope,
    aes(x     = era_label,
        y     = anti_west_foreign,
        group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 1.1,
    alpha     = 0.9
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data  = geo_slope,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_foreign,
        group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color = "#2b6cb0",
    size  = 2.8,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +

  # Left labels — geopolitical only
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = label_left_geo,
    aes(x         = era_label,
        y         = anti_west_foreign,
        label     = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)),
    hjust         = 1,
    direction     = "y",
    nudge_x       = -0.12,
    size          = 2.8,
    color         = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface      = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 10
  ) +

  # Right labels — geopolitical only
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = label_right_geo,
    aes(x         = era_label,
        y         = anti_west_foreign,
        label     = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)),
    hjust         = 0,
    direction     = "y",
    nudge_x       = 0.12,
    size          = 2.8,
    color         = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface      = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 10
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(
    expand = expansion(add = c(2.2, 2.5))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Anti-West framing among foreign experts rose sharply\nafter the 2014 editorial transformation",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Blue lines = geopolitical topics (individual). ",
      "Grey band = domestic / other topics (median \u00B1 interquartile range).\n",
      "Both groups rose after 2014, but geopolitical topics drove the increase."
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Foreign expert anti-West sentiment rate",
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024. Topics with fewer than 20 foreign expert mentions per era excluded."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold",
                                      color = "grey30")
  )

p_slope_clean

ggsave(
  "figure_slope_clean.png",
  plot   = p_slope_clean,
  width  = 11,
  height = 7.5,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

## H4: time

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)

# --- Base era data with counts ---

era_topic_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    era = case_when(
      year < 2014                ~ "1. Pre-2014",
      year >= 2014 & year < 2018 ~ "2. 2014-2017",
      year >= 2018 & year < 2022 ~ "3. 2018-2021",
      year >= 2022               ~ "4. 2022-2024"
    ),
    era = factor(era, levels = c(
      "1. Pre-2014",
      "2. 2014-2017",
      "3. 2018-2021",
      "4. 2022-2024"
    ))
  ) %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean, era) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total            = n(),
    n_foreign          = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic         = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    foreign_share      = n_foreign / n_total,
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_foreign >= 20) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    is_outlier = dominant_topic_clean %in%
                   c("Media and society", "General news"),
    era_num = as.numeric(era),
    era_label = recode(as.character(era),
      "1. Pre-2014"   = "Pre-2014",
      "2. 2014-2017"  = "2014\u20132017",
      "3. 2018-2021"  = "2018\u20132021",
      "4. 2022-2024"  = "2022\u20132024"
    ),
    era_label = factor(era_label, levels = c(
      "Pre-2014", "2014\u20132017", "2018\u20132021", "2022\u20132024"
    ))
  )

# --- Geopolitical group aggregate per era ---

geo_group <- era_topic_stats %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Geopolitical") %>%
  group_by(era_label) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign_total   = sum(n_foreign),
    anti_west_rate    = sum(anti_west_foreign * n_foreign,
                            na.rm = TRUE) / sum(n_foreign),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- All negative sentiment group aggregate per era ---
# Recompute from master_dataset to get anti_russia and anti_ukraine

era_neg_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic",
         foreign_expert == 1) %>%
  mutate(
    era = case_when(
      year < 2014                ~ "1. Pre-2014",
      year >= 2014 & year < 2018 ~ "2. 2014-2017",
      year >= 2018 & year < 2022 ~ "3. 2018-2021",
      year >= 2022               ~ "4. 2022-2024"
    ),
    era = factor(era, levels = c(
      "1. Pre-2014", "2. 2014-2017",
      "3. 2018-2021", "4. 2022-2024"
    ))
  ) %>%
  group_by(era) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign       = n(),
    anti_west_rate  = mean(anti_west,    na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_russia_rate = mean(anti_russia, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_ukraine_rate = mean(anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    any_negative_rate = mean(
      anti_west | anti_russia | anti_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    era_label = recode(as.character(era),
      "1. Pre-2014"   = "Pre-2014",
      "2. 2014-2017"  = "2014\u20132017",
      "3. 2018-2021"  = "2018\u20132021",
      "4. 2022-2024"  = "2022\u20132024"
    ),
    era_label = factor(era_label, levels = c(
      "Pre-2014", "2014\u20132017", "2018\u20132021", "2022\u20132024"
    ))
  )

# --- Shared components ---

geo_slope   <- era_topic_stats %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Geopolitical", !is_outlier)

dom_band <- era_topic_stats %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Domestic / other", !is_outlier) %>%
  group_by(era_label) %>%
  summarise(
    median_rate = median(anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE),
    q25         = quantile(anti_west_foreign, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q75         = quantile(anti_west_foreign, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

label_left_geo  <- geo_slope %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  filter(era_num == min(era_num)) %>%
  ungroup()

label_right_geo <- geo_slope %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  filter(era_num == max(era_num)) %>%
  ungroup()

# --- PLOT 1: Individual geopolitical topics with n labels ---

p_slope_n <- ggplot() +

  # Domestic band
  geom_ribbon(
    data = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, group = 1),
    fill  = "#aab8c2",
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data      = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate, group = 1),
    color     = "#aab8c2",
    linewidth = 1,
    linetype  = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data  = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate),
    color = "#aab8c2",
    size  = 2.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 4.18,
    y = dom_band$median_rate[dom_band$era_label == "2022\u20132024"],
    label    = "Domestic / other\n(median \u00B1 IQR)",
    color    = "#8a9ba8",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # 2014 line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1.5,
    color      = "#b04a4a",
    linewidth  = 0.7,
    alpha      = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 1.53,
    y        = max(geo_slope$anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.97,
    label    = "2014 editorial\ntransformation",
    color    = "#b04a4a",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Geopolitical individual lines
  geom_line(
    data = geo_slope,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_foreign,
        group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 1.1,
    alpha     = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data  = geo_slope,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_foreign,
        group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color = "#2b6cb0",
    size  = 2.8
  ) +

  # N labels above each geopolitical dot
  geom_text(
    data = geo_slope,
    aes(x     = era_label,
        y     = anti_west_foreign,
        group = dominant_topic_clean,
        label = paste0("n=", comma(n_foreign))),
    vjust    = -1.1,
    size     = 2.3,
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Left and right labels
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = label_left_geo,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_foreign,
        label = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)),
    hjust         = 1,
    direction     = "y",
    nudge_x       = -0.12,
    size          = 2.8,
    color         = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface      = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 10
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = label_right_geo,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_foreign,
        label = as.character(dominant_topic_clean)),
    hjust         = 0,
    direction     = "y",
    nudge_x       = 0.12,
    size          = 2.8,
    color         = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface      = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 10
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(2.2, 2.8))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.12))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Anti-West framing among foreign experts rose sharply\nafter the 2014 editorial transformation",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Blue lines = geopolitical topics (individual). ",
      "Grey band = domestic topics (median \u00B1 IQR). ",
      "n = foreign expert mentions per dot."
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Foreign expert anti-West sentiment rate",
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold",
                                      color = "grey30")
  )

p_slope_n

ggsave("figure_slope_with_n.png", plot = p_slope_n,
       width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

# --- PLOT 2: Geopolitical group aggregate vs domestic band ---

p_slope_geo_group <- ggplot() +

  # Domestic band
  geom_ribbon(
    data = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, group = 1),
    fill  = "#aab8c2",
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data      = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate, group = 1),
    color     = "#aab8c2",
    linewidth = 1,
    linetype  = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data  = dom_band,
    aes(x = era_label, y = median_rate),
    color = "#aab8c2",
    size  = 2.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 4.18,
    y        = dom_band$median_rate[dom_band$era_label == "2022\u20132024"],
    label    = "Domestic / other\n(median \u00B1 IQR)",
    color    = "#8a9ba8",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # 2014 line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1.5,
    color      = "#b04a4a",
    linewidth  = 0.7,
    alpha      = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 1.53,
    y        = max(geo_group$anti_west_rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.97,
    label    = "2014 editorial\ntransformation",
    color    = "#b04a4a",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Geopolitical GROUP line
  geom_line(
    data      = geo_group,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_rate, group = 1),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data  = geo_group,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_rate),
    color = "#2b6cb0",
    size  = 4
  ) +

  # N labels above group dots
  geom_text(
    data = geo_group,
    aes(x = era_label, y = anti_west_rate,
        label = paste0("n=", comma(n_foreign_total))),
    vjust    = -1.1,
    size     = 2.8,
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Right label for geopolitical group
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 4.18,
    y        = geo_group$anti_west_rate[geo_group$era_label == "2022\u20132024"],
    label    = "Geopolitical\n(combined)",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(1.5, 2.8))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Geopolitical topics as a group drove the post-2014\nrise in foreign expert anti-West framing",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Blue line = all geopolitical topics combined (weighted average). ",
      "Grey band = domestic topics (median \u00B1 IQR).\n",
      "n = total foreign expert mentions across all geopolitical topics per era."
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Foreign expert anti-West sentiment rate",
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold",
                                      color = "grey30")
  )

p_slope_geo_group

ggsave("figure_slope_geo_group.png", plot = p_slope_geo_group,
       width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

# --- PLOT 3: All negative sentiment targets combined ---
# Anti-West, anti-Russia, anti-Ukraine as separate lines
# Plus any-negative as a combined line

neg_long <- era_neg_stats %>%
  select(era_label, n_foreign,
         anti_west_rate, anti_russia_rate,
         anti_ukraine_rate, any_negative_rate) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_rate, anti_russia_rate,
                  anti_ukraine_rate, any_negative_rate),
    names_to  = "sentiment_type",
    values_to = "rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_label = recode(sentiment_type,
      "anti_west_rate"    = "Anti-West",
      "anti_russia_rate"  = "Anti-Russia",
      "anti_ukraine_rate" = "Anti-Ukraine",
      "any_negative_rate" = "Any negative"
    ),
    sentiment_label = factor(sentiment_label, levels = c(
      "Any negative", "Anti-West", "Anti-Ukraine", "Anti-Russia"
    ))
  )

# Color scheme for sentiment types
sentiment_colors <- c(
  "Any negative"  = "#1a3a5c",
  "Anti-West"     = "#2b6cb0",
  "Anti-Ukraine"  = "#e07b39",
  "Anti-Russia"   = "#b04a4a"
)

sentiment_ltype <- c(
  "Any negative"  = "solid",
  "Anti-West"     = "solid",
  "Anti-Ukraine"  = "solid",
  "Anti-Russia"   = "solid"
)

# Right-side labels
label_right_neg <- neg_long %>%
  group_by(sentiment_label) %>%
  filter(era_label == "2022\u20132024") %>%
  ungroup()

p_slope_neg <- ggplot(neg_long,
                      aes(x         = era_label,
                          y         = rate,
                          color     = sentiment_label,
                          linetype  = sentiment_label,
                          group     = sentiment_label)) +

  # 2014 line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 1.5,
    color      = "#b04a4a",
    linewidth  = 0.7,
    alpha      = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 1.53,
    y        = max(neg_long$rate, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.97,
    label    = "2014 editorial\ntransformation",
    color    = "#b04a4a",
    size     = 2.8,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  geom_line(
    aes(linewidth = sentiment_label == "Any negative")
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +

  # N labels above "Any negative" dots only to avoid clutter
  geom_text(
    data = neg_long %>% filter(sentiment_label == "Any negative"),
    aes(label = paste0("n=", comma(n_foreign))),
    vjust    = -1.1,
    size     = 2.5,
    color    = "#1a3a5c",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Right-side labels
  geom_text_repel(
    data          = label_right_neg,
    aes(label     = sentiment_label),
    hjust         = 0,
    direction     = "y",
    nudge_x       = 0.12,
    size          = 2.8,
    fontface      = "bold",
    segment.color = "grey80",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    max.overlaps  = 10
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = sentiment_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_linetype_manual(
    values = sentiment_ltype,
    guide  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_linewidth_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = 1.8, "FALSE" = 1.0),
    guide  = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(1.5, 2.5))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.12))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign experts became more negative toward all geopolitical\ntargets after 2014, with the West receiving the most criticism",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Rates shown for foreign experts only across all topics. ",
      "'Any negative' = at least one negative evaluation of any target per mention.\n",
      "n = total foreign expert mentions per era."
    ),
    x       = NULL,
    y       = "Share of foreign expert mentions with negative sentiment",
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold",
                                      color = "grey30")
  )

p_slope_neg

ggsave("figure_slope_neg_sentiment.png", plot = p_slope_neg,
       width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

## H2: 

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# --- Data ---

plot_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign          = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic         = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean,
                                       anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Separator ---

topic_levels  <- levels(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean)
geo_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean[
                           plot_data$topic_type == "Geopolitical"
                         ]))
dom_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean[
                           plot_data$topic_type == "Domestic / other"
                         ]))

separator_y <- min(geo_positions) - 0.5
geo_label_y <- separator_y + 0.45
dom_label_y <- separator_y - 0.45

label_x <- max(plot_data$anti_west_foreign) * 0.95

# --- Overall foreign expert anti-West rate ---

overall_rate <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(foreign_expert == 1,
         dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(anti_west, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(rate)

# --- Plot ---

p <- ggplot(plot_data,
            aes(y = dominant_topic_clean)) +

  # Connector line between domestic tick and foreign dot
  geom_segment(
    aes(x    = anti_west_domestic,
        xend = anti_west_foreign,
        yend = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey82",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    linetype  = "dotted"
  ) +

  # Domestic rate — red tick for reference
  geom_point(
    aes(x = anti_west_domestic),
    shape = 124,
    size  = 4,
    color = "#b04a4a",
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  # Foreign expert rate — primary story
  geom_point(
    aes(x     = anti_west_foreign,
        color = topic_type),
    size  = 3.5,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +

  # Percentage label next to each foreign dot
  geom_text(
    aes(x     = anti_west_foreign,
        label = paste0(round(anti_west_foreign * 100, 1), "%")),
    hjust    = -0.35,
    size     = 2.8,
    color    = "grey35"
  ) +

  # Overall foreign expert average reference line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = overall_rate,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth  = 0.5,
    alpha      = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = overall_rate + 0.001,
    y        = 1.2,
    label    = "Overall foreign\nexpert average",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 2.6,
    hjust    = 0,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +

  # Separator line between geopolitical and domestic
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = separator_y,
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.6,
    linetype   = "solid"
  ) +

  # Section label: just above separator
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = label_x,
    y        = geo_label_y,
    label    = "Geopolitical \u25B2",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  # Section label: just below separator
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = label_x,
    y        = dom_label_y,
    label    = "Domestic / other \u25BC",
    color    = "#718096",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Geopolitical"     = "#2b6cb0",
               "Domestic / other" = "#718096"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, NA),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.18))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign experts are more anti-West than domestic experts\nin every topic, and most so in geopolitical ones",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Dot = foreign expert anti-West rate per topic. ",
      "Red tick | = domestic expert rate for reference.\n",
      "Dashed line = overall foreign expert anti-West rate across all topics."
    ),
    x       = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y       = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024. Topics ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate (descending)."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  "figure_antiwest_by_topic.png",
  plot   = p,
  width  = 10,
  height = 7.5,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# --- Data ---

plot_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign          = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic         = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean,
                                       anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Separator ---

topic_levels  <- levels(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean)
geo_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean[
                           plot_data$topic_type == "Geopolitical"
                         ]))
dom_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(plot_data$dominant_topic_clean[
                           plot_data$topic_type == "Domestic / other"
                         ]))

separator_y <- min(geo_positions) - 0.5
geo_label_y <- separator_y + 0.45
dom_label_y <- separator_y - 0.45

# --- Pivot to long for paired bars ---

plot_long <- plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic"),
    expert_label = factor(expert_label,
                          levels = c("Foreign", "Domestic"))
  )

# Rate labels: only on the foreign bar
rate_labels <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0(round(anti_west_foreign * 100, 1), "%")
  )

# Max x value for section label placement
max_x <- max(plot_data$anti_west_foreign, na.rm = TRUE)

# --- Plot ---

p <- ggplot(plot_long,
            aes(x    = anti_west_rate,
                y    = dominant_topic_clean,
                fill = expert_label)) +

  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width    = 0.6,
    alpha    = 0.9
  ) +

  # Rate label at end of foreign bar only
  geom_text(
    data        = rate_labels,
    aes(x     = anti_west_foreign,
        y     = dominant_topic_clean,
        label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust       = -0.2,
    nudge_y     = 0.22,   # offset upward to sit above the foreign bar
    size        = 2.8,
    color       = "#2b6cb0"
  ) +

  # Separator line
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = separator_y,
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.6,
    linetype   = "solid"
  ) +

  # Section label just above separator
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = max_x * 0.98,
    y        = geo_label_y,
    label    = "Geopolitical \u25B2",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  # Section label just below separator
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = max_x * 0.98,
    y        = dom_label_y,
    label    = "Domestic / other \u25BC",
    color    = "#718096",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.20))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign experts are more anti-West than domestic experts\nin every topic, and most so in geopolitical ones",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Blue bar = foreign expert anti-West rate. ",
      "Red bar = domestic expert anti-West rate.\n",
      "Topics ordered by foreign expert anti-West rate (descending)."
    ),
    x       = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y       = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13,
                                      lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                      lineheight = 1.4),
    plot.caption       = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 8),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey93"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  "figure_paired_bars_antiwest.png",
  plot   = p,
  width  = 10,
  height = 7.5,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggalluvial)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# --- Helper function ---

make_alluvial <- function(data, topic_filter, title_label, n_label) {

  flow_data <- data %>%
    filter(topic_type == topic_filter,
           # Only keep mentions with at least one evaluative sentiment
           (anti_west == 1 | anti_russia == 1)) %>%
    mutate(
      expert_label = if_else(foreign_expert == 1, "Foreign", "Domestic"),
      sentiment = case_when(
        anti_west   == 1 ~ "Anti-West",
        anti_russia == 1 ~ "Anti-Russia",
        TRUE             ~ "Other"
      )
    ) %>%
    count(expert_label, sentiment) %>%
    mutate(
      expert_label = factor(expert_label,
                            levels = c("Foreign", "Domestic")),
      sentiment    = factor(sentiment,
                            levels = c("Anti-West", "Anti-Russia"))
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(sentiment))

  ggplot(flow_data,
         aes(axis1 = expert_label,
             axis2 = sentiment,
             y     = n)) +

    geom_alluvium(
      aes(fill = sentiment),
      alpha      = 0.8,
      curve_type = "cubic"
    ) +

    geom_stratum(
      width     = 0.35,
      fill      = "grey96",
      color     = "grey65",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ) +

    geom_text(
      stat     = "stratum",
      aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),
      size     = 3.5,
      fontface = "bold",
      color    = "grey20"
    ) +

    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "Anti-West"   = "#2b6cb0",
        "Anti-Russia" = "#b04a4a"
      ),
      name = NULL
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      limits = c("Expert origin", "Sentiment"),
      expand = expansion(add = c(0.4, 0.4))
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format()) +

    labs(
      title    = title_label,
      subtitle = paste0("n = ", n_label, " evaluative expert mentions"),
      x        = NULL,
      y        = "Expert mentions"
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16,
                                     color = "grey15",
                                     lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 14),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid      = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold",
                                     color = "grey40"),
      axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 12, color = "grey50")
    )
}

# --- Add topic_type to master_dataset for aggregation ---

master_plot <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    )
  )

# --- N counts for subtitles ---

n_geo <- master_plot %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Geopolitical",
         (anti_west == 1 | anti_russia == 1)) %>%
  nrow() %>%
  comma()

n_dom <- master_plot %>%
  filter(topic_type == "Domestic / other",
         (anti_west == 1 | anti_russia == 1)) %>%
  nrow() %>%
  comma()

# --- Build panels ---

p_geo <- make_alluvial(
  data         = master_plot,
  topic_filter = "Geopolitical",
  title_label  = "Geopolitical topics",
  n_label      = n_geo
)

p_dom <- make_alluvial(
  data         = master_plot,
  topic_filter = "Domestic / other",
  title_label  = "Domestic / other topics",
  n_label      = n_dom
)

# --- Combine ---

p_combined <- p_geo + p_dom +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1) +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "Anti-West framing flows through foreign experts;\nanti-Russia framing flows through domestic experts",
    caption  = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024.",
    theme    = theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16,
                                   lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9.5,
                                   lineheight = 1.4),
      plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 12)
    )
  )

p_combined

ggsave(
  "figure_alluvial_two_panel.png",
  plot   = p_combined,
  width  = 12,
  height = 7,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)


```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# --- Data ---

topic_stats <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign          = sum(foreign_expert == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_domestic         = sum(foreign_expert == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 1],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_foreign,
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west[foreign_expert == 0],
                             na.rm = TRUE) / n_domestic,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_type = case_when(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% c(
        "Ukraine war", "International geopolitics",
        "Military technology", "Energy and sanctions"
      ) ~ "Geopolitical",
      TRUE ~ "Domestic / other"
    ),
    dominant_topic_clean = fct_reorder(dominant_topic_clean,
                                       anti_west_foreign)
  )

# --- Aggregate summary rows ---

summary_rows <- topic_stats %>%
  group_by(topic_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_foreign          = sum(n_foreign),
    n_domestic         = sum(n_domestic),
    anti_west_foreign  = sum(anti_west_foreign * n_foreign,
                             na.rm = TRUE) / sum(n_foreign),
    anti_west_domestic = sum(anti_west_domestic * n_domestic,
                             na.rm = TRUE) / sum(n_domestic),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = if_else(
      topic_type == "Geopolitical",
      "Geopolitical\n(combined)",
      "Domestic / other\n(combined)"
    )
  )

# --- Separator position ---

topic_levels  <- levels(topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean)
geo_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean[
                           topic_stats$topic_type == "Geopolitical"
                         ]))
dom_positions <- which(topic_levels %in%
                         as.character(topic_stats$dominant_topic_clean[
                           topic_stats$topic_type == "Domestic / other"
                         ]))

separator_y <- min(geo_positions) - 0.5
geo_label_y <- separator_y + 0.45
dom_label_y <- separator_y - 0.45

# --- Summary panel ---

summary_right_data <- summary_rows %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

p_sum <- ggplot(summary_right_data,
                aes(x     = anti_west_rate,
                    y     = dominant_topic_clean,
                    color = expert_label,
                    shape = expert_label)) +
  geom_line(
    aes(group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.95) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(anti_west_rate * 100, 1), "%")),
    vjust    = -1.1,
    size     = 3.6,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = 16, "Domestic" = 15),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20)),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Evaluation",
    subtitle = "Aggregated anti-West rate by topic group",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14,
                                      color = "#2b6cb0"),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold",
                                      hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position    = "none",   # suppress legend in top panel
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.margin        = margin(b = 4)
  )

# --- Main panel ---

right_data <- topic_stats %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(anti_west_foreign, anti_west_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "anti_west_rate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = if_else(expert_type == "anti_west_foreign",
                           "Foreign", "Domestic")
  )

annotation_topic <- "COVID-19 and health"

p_main <- ggplot(right_data,
                 aes(x     = anti_west_rate,
                     y     = dominant_topic_clean,
                     color = expert_label,
                     shape = expert_label)) +
  geom_line(
    aes(group = dominant_topic_clean),
    color     = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = separator_y,
    color      = "grey40",
    linewidth  = 0.6,
    linetype   = "solid"
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = max(topic_stats$anti_west_foreign) * 0.98,
    y        = geo_label_y,
    label    = "Geopolitical \u25B2",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3.8,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 0,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = max(topic_stats$anti_west_foreign) * 0.98,
    y        = dom_label_y,
    label    = "Domestic / other \u25BC",
    color    = "#718096",
    size     = 3.8,
    hjust    = 1,
    vjust    = 1,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "segment",
    x         = 0.004,
    xend      = 0.016,
    y         = annotation_topic,
    yend      = annotation_topic,
    arrow     = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    color     = "#2b6cb0",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = 0.017,
    y        = annotation_topic,
    label    = "Foreign always\nhigher",
    color    = "#2b6cb0",
    size     = 3.2,
    hjust    = 0,
    vjust    = 0.5,
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = "#2b6cb0", "Domestic" = "#b04a4a"),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic"),
    guide  = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(shape = c(16, 15), size = 4, label = "")
    )
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Foreign" = 16, "Domestic" = 15),
    name   = NULL,
    breaks = c("Foreign", "Domestic"),
    guide  = guide_legend(
      override.aes = list(shape = c(16, 15), size = 4, label = "")
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.20)),
    limits = c(0, NA)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = NULL,
    subtitle = "Anti-West sentiment rate by topic and expert origin",
    x        = "Share of expert mentions with anti-West sentiment",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position    = "bottom",  # legend only here
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

# --- Combine ---

p_final <- p_sum / p_main +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 5)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title   = "Foreign experts are more anti-West than domestic experts\nin every topic, with the gap largest in geopolitical coverage",
    caption = "Source: Lenta.ru corpus 1999\u20132024.",
    theme   = theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 17,
                                  lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.caption = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 12)
    )
  )

p_final

ggsave(
  "figure_evaluation_only.png",
  plot   = p_final,
  width  = 9,
  height = 10,
  dpi    = 300,
  bg     = "white"
)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Panel A: Selection (H1) ────────────────────────────────────────────────

pa_m1 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ topic_group,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

pa_m2 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ topic_group + year,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

pa_m3 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ topic_group * year,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

panel_a_models <- list(
  "M1" = pa_m1,
  "M2" = pa_m2,
  "M3" = pa_m3
)

panel_a_rows <- tribble(
  ~term,            ~M1,  ~M2,  ~M3,
  "Baseline topic", "Crime", "Crime", "Crime",
  "Baseline expert", "Domestic", "Domestic", "Domestic"
)

modelsummary(
  panel_a_models,
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = c(
    "topic_groupgeopolitics"          = "Topic: Geopolitical",
    "topic_groupeconomy"              = "Topic: Economy",
    "topic_grouppolitics"             = "Topic: Politics",
    "year"                            = "Year",
    "topic_groupgeopolitics:year"     = "Geopolitical × Year",
    "topic_groupeconomy:year"         = "Economy × Year",
    "topic_grouppolitics:year"        = "Politics × Year"
  ),
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "Panel A: Dependent variable = Foreign expert presence (H1)",
  notes      = "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses. Baseline: crime topic, domestic expert. * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001",
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Foreign expert presence" = 3))


# ── Panel B: Evaluation (H2) ───────────────────────────────────────────────

pb_spec <- anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year

pb_aw  <- glm(anti_west      ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
              data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
pb_au  <- glm(anti_ukraine   ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
              data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
pb_ar  <- glm(anti_russia    ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
              data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
pb_pw  <- glm(any_positive_west   ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
              data = master_dataset, family = binomial)
pb_pr  <- glm(any_positive_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_group + year,
              data = master_dataset, family = binomial)

panel_b_models <- list(
  "Anti-West"     = pb_aw,
  "Anti-Ukraine"  = pb_au,
  "Anti-Russia"   = pb_ar,
  "Pro-West"      = pb_pw,
  "Pro-Russia"    = pb_pr
)

modelsummary(
  panel_b_models,
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = c(
    "foreign_expert"             = "Foreign expert",
    "topic_groupgeopolitics"     = "Topic: Geopolitical",
    "topic_groupeconomy"         = "Topic: Economy",
    "topic_grouppolitics"        = "Topic: Politics",
    "year"                       = "Year"
  ),
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "Panel B: Dependent variable = Sentiment indicators (H2)",
  notes      = "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses. Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001",
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Negative evaluations" = 3, "Positive evaluations" = 2)) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#f0f4f8")


# ── Panel C: Cross-topic diffusion (H3) ───────────────────────────────────

pc_m1 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

pc_m2 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group + year,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

pc_m3 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_group + year +
               foreign_expert * year,
             data   = master_dataset,
             family = binomial)

panel_c_models <- list(
  "M1" = pc_m1,
  "M2" = pc_m2,
  "M3" = pc_m3
)

modelsummary(
  panel_c_models,
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = c(
    "foreign_expert"                          = "Foreign expert",
    "topic_groupgeopolitics"                  = "Topic: Geopolitical",
    "topic_groupeconomy"                      = "Topic: Economy",
    "topic_grouppolitics"                     = "Topic: Politics",
    "year"                                    = "Year",
    "foreign_expert:topic_groupgeopolitics"   = "Foreign expert × Geopolitical",
    "foreign_expert:topic_groupeconomy"       = "Foreign expert × Economy",
    "foreign_expert:topic_grouppolitics"      = "Foreign expert × Politics",
    "foreign_expert:year"                     = "Foreign expert × Year"
  ),
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "Panel C: Dependent variable = Anti-West sentiment (H3)",
  notes      = "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses. Baseline: domestic expert, crime topic. The foreign expert coefficient captures the effect in the baseline (crime) topic. Interaction terms show how this effect differs in other topic groups. * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001",
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Anti-West sentiment" = 3)) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#f0f4f8") %>%
  row_spec(6, bold = TRUE, background = "#f0f4f8")
```

# Regression models with confidence 

## H1 - regression models 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Data preparation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = relevel(
      factor(dominant_topic_clean),
      ref = "Courts and crime"
    ),
    post_2014 = as.integer(year >= 2014),
    post_2022 = as.integer(year >= 2022)
  )

h1_data_restricted <- h1_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85)

# ── Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_m1 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

h1_m2 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

h1_m4 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
             data   = h1_data_restricted,
             family = binomial)

h1_m5 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + post_2014 + post_2022,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

# ── Coefficient map in block order ─────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map <- c(
  # Geopolitical block
  "dominant_topic_cleanInternational geopolitics" = "International geopolitics",
  "dominant_topic_cleanUkraine war"               = "Ukraine war",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMilitary technology"       = "Military technology",
  "dominant_topic_cleanEnergy and sanctions"      = "Energy and sanctions",
  # Domestic block
  "dominant_topic_cleanMacroeconomy"              = "Macroeconomy",
  "dominant_topic_cleanOpinion and commentary"    = "Opinion and commentary",
  "dominant_topic_cleanInformation services"      = "Information services",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMedia and society"         = "Media and society",
  "dominant_topic_cleanKremlin governance"        = "Kremlin governance",
  "dominant_topic_cleanHousing market"            = "Housing market",
  "dominant_topic_cleanDevelopment and education" = "Development and education",
  "dominant_topic_cleanCOVID-19 and health"       = "COVID-19 and health",
  "dominant_topic_cleanGeneral news"              = "General news",
  "dominant_topic_cleanRegional and local issues" = "Regional and local issues",
  # Controls
  "year"      = "Year (linear)",
  "post_2014" = "Post-2014",
  "post_2022" = "Post-2022"
)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "No controls"              = h1_m1,
    "+ Year (linear)"          = h1_m2,
    "Restricted (conf. 0.85+)" = h1_m4,
    "+ Era indicators"         = h1_m5
  ),
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = coef_map,
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "H1: Foreign expert presence across topics",
  notes      = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic, domestic expert.",
    "Restricted column uses observations where expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85.",
    "Era indicators: Post-2014 and Post-2022 are binary variables marking",
    "the editorial transformation and the full-scale invasion respectively.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Geopolitical topics",
    start_row  = 1,
    end_row    = 4,
    bold       = TRUE,
    background = "#e8f0f7"
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Domestic / other topics",
    start_row  = 5,
    end_row    = 14,
    bold       = TRUE,
    background = "#f7f0e8"
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Controls",
    start_row  = 15,
    end_row    = 17,
    bold       = FALSE,
    background = "#f5f5f5"
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "           = 1,
      "Main models" = 2,
      "Robustness"  = 2)
  ) %>%
  column_spec(4, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(5, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)


library(broom)

# Extract tidy results from all three models
m1_tidy <- tidy(h1_m1) %>% mutate(model = "No controls")
m2_tidy <- tidy(h1_m2) %>% mutate(model = "+ Year")
m4_tidy <- tidy(h1_m4) %>% mutate(model = "Robustness")

all_results <- bind_rows(m1_tidy, m2_tidy, m4_tidy) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, p.value)

print(all_results, n = 100)

summary(h1_m2)
summary(h1_m4)
```

## H2 - Regression model

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)
```
